```
SaveDashboardLayoutAsync(context, layout)           → DataProcessResult[Dictionary]
```

### FREEDOM Configuration
| Parameter | Default | Configurable |
|-----------|---------|-------------|
| Dashboard cache TTL | 30s | Yes — per dashboard |
| Max query time range | 90 days | Yes — per tenant tier |
| Max series per query | 20 | Yes — per subscription |

### MACHINE Rules
- ALL queries MUST include tenantId scope (DNA-5) — no cross-tenant metric access
- Dashboard queries MUST use Redis cache (F520 Redis layer) — no raw ES on every call
- Query timeout MUST be enforced (hard cap: 30s, FREEDOM: configurable lower)

```

ResolveErrorGroupAsync(context, fingerprintId)      → DataProcessResult[bool]

```

| Parameter | Default | Configurable |
|-----------|---------|-------------|
| Error grouping algorithm | stack-hash | Yes — message-hash or custom |

### MACHINE Rules
- Error payloads MUST be PII-scrubbed before storage (integration point: F462 PII classifier)
- Error grouping fingerprint MUST be deterministic (same error → same group always)
- tenantId on all error records (DNA-5)

| Max alerts per hour | 10 | Yes — per tenant |

### MACHINE Rules
- Alert evaluation MUST be async (never block ingestion pipeline)
- Alert channel delivery MUST go through QUEUE FABRIC — never direct HTTP in evaluation path

# FAMILY 70 — EXTERNAL INTEGRATIONS

## Context
Family 70 enables the engine to generate integration connectors: OAuth2 exchange,
webhook relay, Zapier, Google Drive, Salesforce, Slack, email. These sit on top
of FLOW-14's connector authentication plane (F426–F431), reusing the credential
vault and rate limit guard patterns.

**Upstream dependencies:**
- FLOW-14 F427 (ICredentialVaultService) — OAuth token storage
- FLOW-14 F430 (IRateLimitGuardService) — external API rate limiting
- FLOW-14 F429 (IWebhookReceiverService) — inbound webhook base pattern
- FLOW-15 F507 (IInvoicingService) — billing for integration usage (enterprise tier)

**Design invariants for Family 70:**
- ALL OAuth tokens MUST be stored in FLOW-14 F427 (ICredentialVaultService) — never F528 directly
- EVERY external API call MUST be guarded by FLOW-14 F430 (IRateLimitGuardService) — no exceptions
- Webhook relay MUST verify HMAC/signature before processing (CF-239 guard)
- Each connector MUST have F535 (health check) and F536 (audit log) entries

---

## F527 — IIntegrationCatalogService

**Interface:** `IIntegrationCatalogService`
**Family:** 70 — External Integrations
**Factory ID:** F527

### Fabric Resolution
| Fabric | Provider | Usage |
|--------|----------|-------|
| DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) | Elasticsearch | Integration catalog, installed integrations index |
| QUEUE FABRIC (SK-383 (RedisStreamQueue)) | Redis Streams | IntegrationInstalled, IntegrationRemoved, IntegrationUpdated |

### Methods (all return `DataProcessResult[T]`)
```
ListAvailableIntegrationsAsync(context, filter)     → DataProcessResult<list[Dictionary]>
GetIntegrationDetailsAsync(context, integrationId)  → DataProcessResult[Dictionary]
InstallIntegrationAsync(context, integrationId, cfg)→ DataProcessResult[Dictionary]
RemoveIntegrationAsync(context, installId)          → DataProcessResult[bool]
ListInstalledAsync(context, tenantFilter)           → DataProcessResult<list[Dictionary]>
GetIntegrationCapabilitiesAsync(context, integId)  → DataProcessResult[Dictionary]
```

### FREEDOM Configuration
| Parameter | Default | Configurable |
|-----------|---------|-------------|
| Max integrations per tenant | 10 | Yes — per subscription tier |
| Integration categories | All | Yes — admin can restrict categories |

## F528 — IOAuthExchangeService

**Interface:** `IOAuthExchangeService`
**Family:** 70 — External Integrations
**Factory ID:** F528

### Fabric Resolution
| Fabric | Provider | Usage |
|--------|----------|-------|
| CORE FABRIC (SK-379 (MicroserviceBase)) | HTTP client | OAuth2 PKCE authorization + token endpoint calls |
| DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) | PostgreSQL (encrypted) | Stores token metadata (NOT the token itself — delegates to F427) |
| QUEUE FABRIC (SK-383 (RedisStreamQueue)) | Redis Streams | OAuthConnected, OAuthRevoked, TokenRefreshFailed |

### Methods (all return `DataProcessResult[T]`)
```
InitiateOAuthFlowAsync(context, integrationId)      → DataProcessResult[Dictionary]
ExchangeCodeAsync(context, integrationId, code)     → DataProcessResult[Dictionary]
RefreshTokenAsync(context, installId)               → DataProcessResult[Dictionary]
RevokeTokenAsync(context, installId)                → DataProcessResult[bool]
GetTokenStatusAsync(context, installId)             → DataProcessResult[Dictionary]
```

### MACHINE Rules
- F528 MUST store token via FLOW-14 F427 (ICredentialVaultService) — never in F528 directly (CF-238)
- OAuth flow MUST use PKCE (code_challenge + code_verifier) — implicit flow forbidden
- Token refresh MUST be attempted before expiry (proactive, not reactive)
- All token operations MUST emit queue events (audit trail)

---

## F529 — IWebhookRelayService

**Interface:** `IWebhookRelayService`
**Family:** 70 — External Integrations
**Factory ID:** F529

### Fabric Resolution
| Fabric | Provider | Usage |
|--------|----------|-------|
| QUEUE FABRIC (SK-383 (RedisStreamQueue)) | Redis Streams | WebhookReceived, WebhookRelayed, WebhookFailed |
| DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) | Elasticsearch | Webhook payload log, delivery attempt history |
| DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) | Redis | HMAC verification cache + dedup (eventId SETNX) |

### Methods (all return `DataProcessResult[T]`)
```
ReceiveWebhookAsync(context, source, payload, sig)  → DataProcessResult[Dictionary]
RelayToAppAsync(context, webhookId, appId)          → DataProcessResult[Dictionary]
GetDeliveryStatusAsync(context, webhookId)          → DataProcessResult[Dictionary]
ListWebhookHistoryAsync(context, filter)            → DataProcessResult<list[Dictionary]>
RetryDeliveryAsync(context, webhookId)              → DataProcessResult[Dictionary]
RegisterWebhookSourceAsync(context, sourcePayload)  → DataProcessResult[Dictionary]
```

### MACHINE Rules
- HMAC/signature MUST be verified before any relay action (CF-239 enforcement)
- Dedup MUST use Redis SETNX with external eventId (prevent float-processing)
- Failed relays MUST be queued to DLQ after 3 attempts (queue pattern)
- Relay MUST NOT expose internal app URLs to the webhook source

# FAMILY 71 — AI ADD-ONS & INTELLIGENT COMPONENTS

## Context
Family 71 enables the engine to generate AI-powered components that users drop
into their generated apps: chatbots, prediction widgets, automation triggers,
insight panels. All AI calls go through AI ENGINE FABRIC (never direct provider).
Token usage feeds F546 → F506 (billing metering from Family 67).

**Upstream dependencies:**
- AI ENGINE FABRIC SK-385/SK-386 (IAiProvider/AiDispatcher) (IAiProvider + AiDispatcher) — all LLM calls
- RAG FABRIC SK-388/SK-389 (IRagService/HybridRag) (IRagService) — context retrieval for chatbot/predictions
- FLOW-15 F496–F500 (Plugin system) — add-ons are a sub-category of plugins
- FLOW-15 F506 (IUsageMeteringService) — AI token usage → billing

**Design invariants for Family 71:**
- ALL AI inferences MUST go through AI ENGINE FABRIC IAiProvider.GenerateAsync() — never openai.chat()
- AI token usage MUST be tracked via F546 and fed to F506 (billing pipeline)
- Add-on execution MUST be sandboxed via F547 (ResourceQuota enforcement)
- Chatbot context MUST NOT leak across tenants (RAG FABRIC scope isolation)

---

| AI ENGINE FABRIC (SK-385/SK-386 (IAiProvider/AiDispatcher)) | IAiProvider | Add-on description embedding for semantic search |

```

GetAddonDetailsAsync(context, addonId)              → DataProcessResult[Dictionary]
RegisterAddonAsync(context, addonPayload)           → DataProcessResult[Dictionary]
DeprecateAddonAsync(context, addonId)               → DataProcessResult[bool]
CheckCompatibilityAsync(context, addonId, appId)    → DataProcessResult[Dictionary]
SearchAddonsByCapabilityAsync(context, query)       → DataProcessResult<list[Dictionary]>
```

### MACHINE Rules
- F538 MUST NOT share an ES index with F496 (IPluginRegistryService) — separate catalog (CF-252)
- Add-on versioning MUST be immutable (no in-place version updates, only new versions)


- App knowledge indexed for chatbot MUST be tenant-scoped (DNA-5)

# FAMILY 72 — INFRASTRUCTURE SCALING & HEALTH

## Context
Family 72 enables the engine to generate infrastructure management services:
auto-scaling decisions, health probes, circuit breakers, backpressure, blue-green
deploys, and capacity planning. The engine generates the scaling logic layer —
the actual infra API calls go through CORE FABRIC.

**Upstream dependencies:**
- FLOW-08 F252–F256 (managed primitives — what gets scaled)
- FLOW-14 F461 (ITenantWarehouseIsolationService) — scaling must respect tenant isolation
- FLOW-15 F508 (IPaywallGateService) — quota-enforced tenants cannot exceed scale bounds
- FLOW-15 F551 (IResourceQuotaService) — scale decisions MUST check quota first

**Design invariants for Family 72:**
- Auto-scale decisions MUST check tenant quota (F551) before scaling up (CF-242)
- Circuit breaker state MUST NOT contradict health probe state (CF-243)
- Blue-green deployment MUST coordinate with F513 (DeployPipeline) and F516 (Rollback)
- All infrastructure operations MUST emit queue events (observability requirement)

---

## F548 — IAutoScalerService

**Interface:** `IAutoScalerService`
**Family:** 72 — Infrastructure Scaling & Health
**Factory ID:** F548

### Fabric Resolution
| Fabric | Provider | Usage |
|--------|----------|-------|
| CORE FABRIC (SK-379 (MicroserviceBase)) | Infra API (k8s HPA equivalent) | Scale-up/down requests to cluster |
| DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) | Elasticsearch | Scaling decision history, metric signals |

### Methods (all return `DataProcessResult[T]`)
```
EvaluateScaleDecisionAsync(context, serviceId, metrics) → DataProcessResult[Dictionary]
ScaleUpAsync(context, serviceId, targetReplicas)    → DataProcessResult[Dictionary]
ScaleDownAsync(context, serviceId, targetReplicas)  → DataProcessResult[Dictionary]
GetCurrentScaleAsync(context, serviceId)            → DataProcessResult[Dictionary]
SetAutoScalePolicyAsync(context, serviceId, policy) → DataProcessResult[Dictionary]
GetScaleHistoryAsync(context, serviceId, window)    → DataProcessResult[Dictionary]
```

### MACHINE Rules
- F548 MUST check F551 (ResourceQuotaService) before every scale-up decision (CF-242)
- Scale-down MUST be gradual (never drop to 0 replicas for live services)
- All scale decisions MUST be queued as events (scale metrics stored in ES)

---


- HALF-OPEN state MUST allow exactly 1 probe call, then transition based on result

# FAMILY 73 — ADVANCED MULTI-TENANT / ENTERPRISE TIER

## Context
Family 73 enables the engine to generate enterprise-grade multi-tenancy features:
silo schema graduation, RLS policy management, BYOK, SLA monitoring, data residency,
enterprise audit, and compliance reporting. Builds on FLOW-14 isolation patterns
and FLOW-11 MT model.

**Upstream dependencies:**
- FLOW-14 F461 (ITenantWarehouseIsolationService) — warehouse isolation model reuse
- FLOW-14 F463 (IRowLevelSecurityService) — RLS base pattern
- FLOW-14 F462 (IPiiClassificationService) — PII before any mart/export
- FLOW-11 SK-61 (MT Isolation pattern) — silo graduation reuse
- DR-65 (Warehouse Isolation inherits FLOW-08 tenant model) — design anchor

**Design invariants for Family 73:**
- Silo graduation MUST migrate data cleanly (no data left in pooled tables) (CF-245)
- F559 RLS policies MUST NOT share namespace with FLOW-14 F463 (CF-246)
- BYOK key rotation MUST trigger PII re-encryption (CF-247)
- F565 enterprise onboarding MUST precede F461 warehouse isolation provision (CF-251)

---

**Factory ID:** F558

| Fabric | Provider | Usage |
|--------|----------|-------|
| DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) | PostgreSQL (schema operations) | Schema creation, data migration, cursor tracking |
| QUEUE FABRIC (SK-383 (RedisStreamQueue)) | Redis Streams | GraduationStarted, GraduationCompleted, GraduationFailed |

### Methods (all return `DataProcessResult[T]`)
```

GetGraduationStatusAsync(context, graduationId)     → DataProcessResult[Dictionary]

RollbackGraduationAsync(context, graduationId)      → DataProcessResult[bool]
```

### MACHINE Rules
- Migration MUST be cursor-based (EP-4 pattern — cursor persisted before batch advance)
- Graduation MUST verify data integrity before cutover (zero-loss guarantee)
- Pooled tables MUST be cleaned after successful graduation (no orphaned data) (CF-245)
- Graduation is irreversible post-cutover (RollbackGraduation only valid pre-cutover)

# NEW ENGINE PRIMITIVES — EP-4 AND EP-5

## EP-4 — Idempotency Key Registry

**Purpose**: Assigns, stores, and validates idempotency keys for all non-idempotent saga
operations. Prevents float-charge, float-refund, float-payout, and float-reservation under
network retries, pod restarts, and saga compensation replays.

**Layer**: FABRIC (not a factory — infrastructure primitive used by factory methods)

**Storage**: Redis with configurable TTL. Financial operations: 24h TTL. Non-financial: 1h TTL.

**Key composition**: `{operationName}:{entityId}:{attemptId}:{tenantId}`
Example: `auth:order-abc123:attempt-1:tenant-xyz`

**State Machine**:
```
[key not found]  → GetOrCreateAsync → NEW
NEW              → MarkInProgressAsync → IN_PROGRESS
IN_PROGRESS      → MarkCompletedAsync(result) → COMPLETED
IN_PROGRESS      → MarkFailedAsync(error) → FAILED
COMPLETED        → GetOrCreateAsync → return cachedResult (no-op, idempotent)
IN_PROGRESS (on retry) → GetOrCreateAsync → 409 Conflict (duplicate in flight)
FAILED           → allow retry with new attempt variant key
```

**Factory Interface** (F578 — IIdempotencyKeyRegistry):
```python
class IIdempotencyKeyRegistry(ABC):
{
    Coroutine[DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]] GetOrCreateAsync(
        str operationKey, TimeSpan ttl, str tenantId)
    Coroutine[DataProcessResult[bool]] MarkInProgressAsync(
        str operationKey, str tenantId)
    Coroutine[DataProcessResult[bool]] MarkCompletedAsync(
        str operationKey, dict[str, Any] result,
        str tenantId)
    Coroutine[DataProcessResult[bool]] MarkFailedAsync(
        str operationKey, str errorCode,
        str tenantId)
}
```

**FABRIC RESOLUTION**: F578 → DATABASE FABRIC (Redis) — TTL-backed key-value store

**When mandatory (DNA-9)**: Any factory method that creates, modifies, or deletes financial state
(payments, refunds, payouts, inventory hard-reservations) or sends an event with external side
effects. AF-7 Compliance station enforces: absence of EP-4 call before financial operation =
BUILD FAILURE.

---

## EP-5 — Transactional Outbox Relay

**Purpose**: Guarantees that domain state changes and their associated domain events are either
BOTH committed or BOTH rolled back. Eliminates the "DB commit succeeded, queue publish failed"
dual-write failure class — which is catastrophic in payment and fulfillment flows.

**Layer**: FABRIC (infrastructure primitive — background relay worker)

**Mechanism**:
1. Factory method opens a PG transaction
2. Domain state written to business table (e.g., orders)
3. Domain event written to `outbox_events` table IN THE SAME TRANSACTION
4. Transaction commits atomically (both or neither)
5. F579 background relay worker polls `outbox_events WHERE relayed_at IS NULL`
6. Relay publishes each row to Queue Fabric (IQueueService.EnqueueAsync)
7. Relay marks `relayed_at = NOW()` — row won't be re-published

**Outbox Table Schema**:
```sql
CREATE TABLE outbox_events (
  outbox_id      UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  tenant_id      VARCHAR(128) NOT NULL,
  event_type     VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
  payload        JSONB NOT NULL,               -- DNA-1: no typed model
  created_at     TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
  relayed_at     TIMESTAMPTZ,                  -- NULL = not yet relayed
  relay_attempts INT DEFAULT 0
)
-- Relay worker index — only unrelayed rows, oldest first
CREATE INDEX idx_outbox_pending
  ON outbox_events (created_at ASC)
  WHERE relayed_at IS NULL
```

**Factory Interface** (F579 — ITransactionalOutboxRelay):
```python
class ITransactionalOutboxRelay(ABC):
{
    # MUST be called within the same IDbTransaction as the domain write
    Coroutine[DataProcessResult[str]] WriteOutboxEventAsync(
        str eventType,
        dict[str, Any] payload,
        str tenantId,
        IDbTransaction transaction)
    # Called by background relay worker only — not by business factory methods
    Coroutine[DataProcessResult[int]] RelayPendingEventsAsync(
        int batchSize, str tenantId)
}
```

**FABRIC RESOLUTION**: F579 → DATABASE FABRIC (PostgreSQL) for outbox table + QUEUE FABRIC
(Redis Streams) for relay publication

**When mandatory (DNA-8 + DR-87)**: All financial events — OrderPlaced, PaymentAuthorized,
PaymentCaptured, PaymentVoided, RefundExecuted, ShipmentCreated, PayoutReleased, PayoutHeld.
AF-7 Compliance station enforces: financial event factory method calling IQueueService directly
(bypassing F579) = BUILD FAILURE.

---

# DNA PATTERN — DNA-9 (NEW)

## DNA-9 — IDEMPOTENCY-FIRST

**Applies to**: Every factory method that creates, modifies, or deletes financial state
(payments, refunds, payouts, inventory hard-reservations) AND every factory method that sends
a message or event with external side effects (carrier notification, payment gateway call).

**Rule**:
```
BEFORE executing any financial factory operation:
  1. Compose operationKey = "{operationName}:{entityId}:{attemptId}:{tenantId}"
  2. Call F578.GetOrCreateAsync(operationKey, ttl, tenantId)
  3. If result.status == COMPLETED → return result.cachedResult (idempotent no-op)
  4. If result.status == IN_PROGRESS → return DataProcessResult.Conflict("duplicate_in_flight")
  5. If result.status == NEW → proceed; call F578.MarkInProgressAsync
  6. Execute the financial operation
  7. Call F578.MarkCompletedAsync(result) OR F578.MarkFailedAsync(error)
```

**TTL Values (FREEDOM config defaults)**:
- Payment operations (authorize, capture, void, refund): `marketplace.idempotency.financial_ttl` = 24h
- Payout operations: `marketplace.idempotency.payout_ttl` = 24h
- Inventory hard-reservation: `marketplace.idempotency.inventory_ttl` = 1h
- Compensation steps: inherit TTL of the step they compensate

**Iron Rule**: Any financial factory method without EP-4 call = BUILD FAILURE.
AF-7 Compliance station validates statically. AF-9 Judge validates at quality gate.

**Evidence / Rationale**: Network timeouts on payment gateway calls cause clients to retry.
Without idempotency: each retry = new authorization = float hold on buyer's payment method.
ST-144 (Duplicate Payment Authorization Retry) validates this pattern.

**Factories where DNA-9 applies**:
F569 (LockCartAsync), F570 (CreateOrderAsync), F571 (all 6 payment methods),
F572 (PromoteToHardReservationAsync, ReleaseReservationAsync), F574 (RecordDecisionAsync),
F575 (ReleasePayoutAsync, HoldPayoutAsync)

**Factories where DNA-9 is N/A**:
F566, F567, F568 (admin/content ops), F573 (logistics tracking — external events),
F576, F577 (read-only search/compute), F578 (IS the DNA-9 implementation), F579 (infrastructure)

---

# FAMILY 74 — GIANT SHOP MARKETPLACE CORE (F566–F579)

## F566 — ISellerAccountService

**Purpose**: Manages seller identity lifecycle: registration, KYC status, store activation,
and performance metric tracking. Entry point for all seller-side marketplace operations.

**FACTORY RESOLUTION**: F566 → DATABASE FABRIC (PostgreSQL primary, Elasticsearch secondary)
+ QUEUE FABRIC (seller.* events)

**Resolved via**: `IExternalServiceFactory<ISellerAccountService>.CreateAsync(ctx)`
- Config key: `marketplace.sellers.provider`
- Fallback: READ-ONLY mode (returns DataProcessResult.ServiceUnavailable)

**Interface**:
```python
class ISellerAccountService(ABC):
{
    Coroutine[DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]] RegisterSellerAsync(
        dict[str, Any] registrationPayload, str tenantId)
    Coroutine[DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]] SubmitKycDocumentsAsync(
        str sellerId, dict[str, Any] kycPayload,
        str tenantId)
    Coroutine[DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]] UpdateKycStatusAsync(
        str sellerId, str newStatus, str reason,
        str tenantId)
    Coroutine[DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]] ActivateStoreAsync(
        str sellerId, str tenantId)
    Coroutine[DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]] UpdatePerformanceMetricsAsync(
        str sellerId, dict[str, Any] metricsPayload,
        str tenantId)
}
```

**DNA Compliance**:
- DNA-1: All payloads dict[str, Any] (no SellerDto, KycDto)
- DNA-3: DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]] on all methods
- DNA-4: Extends MicroserviceBase (19 components)
- DNA-5: tenantId on every method
- DNA-8: seller.registration_submitted, seller.store_activated via F579 outbox

**Primary Fabric Methods Used**: IDatabaseService.StoreDocument, IDatabaseService.SearchDocuments
(build_search_filter — DNA-2), IQueueService.EnqueueAsync (via F579)

## F567 — IProductCatalogService

**Purpose**: Manages product listing lifecycle: draft creation, submission for moderation,
publication to catalog, deactivation, and Elasticsearch indexing for discovery.

PostgreSQL secondary — authoritative listing state) + AI ENGINE FABRIC (listing enrichment)

**Interface**:

```python
class IProductCatalogService(ABC):
{
    Coroutine[DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]] SaveDraftAsync(
        dict[str, Any] listingPayload, str tenantId)
    Coroutine[DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]] SubmitListingAsync(
        str listingId, str tenantId)
    Coroutine[DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]] PublishListingAsync(
        str listingId, str tenantId,
        IDbTransaction transaction); # EP-5
```

DNA-2 (build_search_filter on all ES queries — skips empty filters automatically)

**Key Constraint**: CF-267 — every SearchDocuments call from F567 injects mandatory
`listing_status: PUBLISHED` filter. This cannot be overridden via config.

## F568 — IListingModerationService

**Purpose**: AI-powered content screening for both listing moderation (product policy) and
seller KYC document screening. Uses AF-5 multi-model parallel orchestration with 2/3 consensus.

**FACTORY RESOLUTION**: F568 → AI ENGINE FABRIC (IAiProvider / AiDispatcher — 3 parallel
models: Claude, GPT-4o, Gemini) + DATABASE FABRIC (PostgreSQL — ModerationCase records)
+ QUEUE FABRIC (moderation.* events)

**Interface**:
```python
class IListingModerationService(ABC):
{
    Coroutine[DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]] CreateModerationCaseAsync(
        str listingId, dict[str, Any] listingContent,
        str tenantId)
    # Internally calls IAiProvider.GenerateAsync() × 3 via AiDispatcher (AF-5)
    # Returns consensus: {decision, confidence, votes:[], violations:[]}
    Coroutine[DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]] RunModerationConsensusAsync(
        str caseId, dict[str, Any] policyContext,
        str tenantId)
    Coroutine[DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]] RunKycScreeningAsync(
        str sellerId, dict[str, Any] kycDocuments,
        str tenantId)
}
```

**Multi-model Consensus Logic**:
- ≥ 2/3 APPROVE → moderation.approved
- ≥ 2/3 REJECT → moderation.rejected (violations list returned)
- 1/3 split → moderation.human_review → human queue with EP-2 SLA timer

**DNA Compliance**: DNA-1 through DNA-6; DNA-7 (calls IAiProvider.GenerateAsync(), never
openai.chat()); consensus stored as Dictionary (DNA-1)

---

## F569 — ICartStateService

**Purpose**: Ephemeral cart state management with TTL-backed Redis storage. Handles item
add/remove, coupon pre-validation, cart locking (Redis SETNX) during checkout saga, and
cart-to-order conversion.

**FACTORY RESOLUTION**: F569 → DATABASE FABRIC (Redis primary — ephemeral cart state with TTL)
+ DATABASE FABRIC (PostgreSQL secondary — cart audit trail)

**Interface**:
```python
class ICartStateService(ABC):
{
    Coroutine[DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]] AddItemAsync(
        str buyerId, dict[str, Any] item,
        str tenantId)
    Coroutine[DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]] GetCartAsync(
        str buyerId, str tenantId)
    # Redis SETNX — prevents concurrent checkouts for same cart
    Coroutine[DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]] LockCartAsync(
        str buyerId, str correlationId,
        str tenantId)
    # Compensation step for saga rollback
    Coroutine[DataProcessResult[bool]] UnlockCartAsync(
        str buyerId, str tenantId)
    Coroutine[DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]] ConvertToOrderAsync(
        str buyerId, str orderId,
        str tenantId)
}
```

**DNA Compliance**: DNA-1 through DNA-6; DNA-5 (tenantId scopes all Redis keys:
`cart:{tenantId}:{buyerId}`); DNA-9 (LockCartAsync carries idempotency key)

**CF-257 Enforcement**: Cart item TTL = `marketplace.cart.item.ttl_seconds` (FREEDOM config).
BFA validates cart TTL ≤ inventory soft-reservation TTL at startup.

**Interface**:

```python
class IOrderManagementService(ABC):
{

    Coroutine[DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]] CreateOrderAsync(

    Coroutine[DataProcessResult[bool]] CancelOrderAsync(
```

DNA-8 (OrderPlaced, OrderConfirmed, OrderCanceled all via F579.WriteOutboxEventAsync)

## F571 — IPaymentEscrowService

**Purpose**: Full payment lifecycle for marketplace escrow model: authorization → hold/escrow
→ capture (on shipment) → release (after protection window) OR refund (on dispute).
All operations carry EP-4 idempotency keys (DNA-9).

**FACTORY RESOLUTION**: F571 → DATABASE FABRIC (PostgreSQL — authoritative payment state,
Redis — payment status cache for fast reads) + QUEUE FABRIC (payment.* events via F579)

**Interface**:
```python
class IPaymentEscrowService(ABC):
{
    # DNA-9: idempotencyKey required
    Coroutine[DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]] AuthorizePaymentAsync(
        dict[str, Any] paymentPayload, str idempotencyKey,
        str tenantId)
    Coroutine[DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]] EscrowPaymentAsync(
        str paymentId, str orderId,
        str tenantId)
    # Triggered by shipment.created event (CF-261) — DNA-9 required
    Coroutine[DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]] CapturePaymentAsync(
        str paymentId, str idempotencyKey,
        str tenantId)
    # Compensation — DNA-9 required
    Coroutine[DataProcessResult[bool]] VoidAuthorizationAsync(
        str paymentId, str idempotencyKey,
        str tenantId)
    # Triggered by dispute decision (T224) — DNA-9 required
    Coroutine[DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]] RefundAsync(
        str paymentId, float amount, str idempotencyKey,
        str tenantId)
    Coroutine[DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]] GetPaymentStatusAsync(
        str paymentId, str tenantId)
}
```

**DNA Compliance**: DNA-1 through DNA-9 (all 9 patterns — F571 is the highest-compliance
factory in the engine; every financial method carries idempotency key)

**IMPORTANT**: F571 never calls Stripe, Braintree, or any payment gateway directly.
All gateway calls are via IAiProvider equivalent — a pluggable IPaymentGatewayProvider
resolved through the AI ENGINE FABRIC extension point for payment gateways.

## F572 — IInventoryReservationService

**Purpose**: Two-layer inventory reservation: fast soft-reservation in Redis (cart-level),
durable hard-reservation in PostgreSQL (checkout-level). Hard reservation uses SELECT FOR UPDATE
row lock. Prevents overselling under concurrent checkout.

**FACTORY RESOLUTION**: F572 → DATABASE FABRIC (Redis — soft reservation + TTL) +
DATABASE FABRIC (PostgreSQL — hard reservation with row locking + hard constraint)
+ QUEUE FABRIC (inventory.* events)

**Interface**:
```python
class IInventoryReservationService(ABC):
{
    # Fast path — Redis SETNX with TTL
    Coroutine[DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]] SoftReserveAsync(
        str listingId, int quantity, str buyerId,
        str tenantId)
    # Checkout path — releases Redis soft lock, acquires PG row lock
    # DNA-9: idempotencyKey required
    Coroutine[DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]] PromoteToHardReservationAsync(
        str listingId, int quantity, str orderId, str idempotencyKey,
        str tenantId)
    # Compensation — DNA-9 required (CF-260, CF-263, CF-264 all trigger this)
    Coroutine[DataProcessResult[bool]] ReleaseReservationAsync(
        str reservationId, str idempotencyKey,
        str tenantId)
    Coroutine[DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]] GetAvailableStockAsync(
        str listingId, str tenantId)
}
```

**DNA Compliance**: DNA-1 through DNA-9

**CF-260 Enforcement**: PromoteToHardReservationAsync checks
`available_stock = listed_stock − hard_reserved_stock` before acquiring PG row lock.
If insufficient → DataProcessResult.Failure("insufficient_inventory", availableStock).

## F573 — IFulfillmentOrchestrationService

**Purpose**: Manages shipment lifecycle: creation, carrier tracking webhook relay, delivery
confirmation. Publishes fulfillment events via Queue Fabric (EP-5 outbox for financial events).

**FACTORY RESOLUTION**: F573 → QUEUE FABRIC (Redis Streams — shipment events, tracking webhooks)
+ DATABASE FABRIC (PostgreSQL — shipment records, tracking history)

**Interface**:
```python
class IFulfillmentOrchestrationService(ABC):
{
    # shipment.created via F579 outbox (EP-5) — triggers CF-261 → payment capture
    Coroutine[DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]] CreateShipmentAsync(
        str orderId, dict[str, Any] shipmentPayload,
        str tenantId)
    Coroutine[DataProcessResult[bool]] RegisterCarrierTrackingAsync(
        str shipmentId, str trackingNumber, str carrierId,
        str tenantId)
    # Called by carrier tracking webhook relay
    Coroutine[DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]] ProcessTrackingUpdateAsync(
        dict[str, Any] trackingEvent,
        str tenantId)
    # order.delivered → triggers EP-2 protection window (F575)
    Coroutine[DataProcessResult[bool]] RecordDeliveryConfirmationAsync(
        str shipmentId, dict[str, Any] deliveryProof,
        str tenantId)
}
```

**DNA Compliance**: DNA-1 through DNA-6, DNA-8 (shipment.created via F579 outbox for EP-5)

## F574 — IDisputeArbitrationService

**Purpose**: Manages buyer protection dispute lifecycle from opening through evidence collection,
AI-assisted decision support, final decision, and refund execution trigger. Enforces seller
response SLA via EP-2.

**FACTORY RESOLUTION**: F574 → DATABASE FABRIC (PostgreSQL — DisputeCase, evidence records,
decision audit) + AI ENGINE FABRIC (IAiProvider.GenerateAsync — decision support analysis)
+ QUEUE FABRIC (dispute.* events)

**Interface**:
```python
class IDisputeArbitrationService(ABC):
{
    # dispute.opened → CF-262 triggers F575.HoldPayoutAsync
    Coroutine[DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]] OpenDisputeAsync(
        dict[str, Any] disputePayload,
        str tenantId)
    Coroutine[DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]] UploadEvidenceAsync(
        str disputeId, dict[str, Any] evidence,
        str tenantId)
    # Calls IAiProvider.GenerateAsync() for decision support (not binding decision)
    Coroutine[DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]] GetAiDecisionSupportAsync(
        str disputeId, str tenantId)
    # Human decision — DNA-9 idempotencyKey required; triggers refund if applicable
    Coroutine[DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]] RecordDecisionAsync(
        str disputeId, dict[str, Any] decision,
        str idempotencyKey, str tenantId)
}
```

**DNA Compliance**: DNA-1 through DNA-9; DNA-7 (GetAiDecisionSupportAsync calls IAiProvider,
never SDK directly); audit trail stored as Dictionary (DNA-1)

**CF-262 + CF-265**: OpenDisputeAsync publishes dispute.opened → BFA triggers F575.HoldPayoutAsync
→ F575 publishes payout.held → CF-265 enforces seller.payout_hold_notified within 500ms.

## F575 — ISellerPayoutService

**Purpose**: Escrow-style seller payout management. Credits seller wallet on delivery, holds
until protection window expires (EP-2), freezes on dispute (CF-262), and releases after
dispute resolution or window expiry. All payout operations carry EP-4 idempotency keys.

**FACTORY RESOLUTION**: F575 → DATABASE FABRIC (PostgreSQL — payout records, wallet balances,
hold ledger) + QUEUE FABRIC (payout.* events via F579) + DATABASE FABRIC (Redis — payout
status cache)

**Interface**:
```python
class ISellerPayoutService(ABC):
{
    # Called on order.delivered — credits wallet, registers EP-2 protection timer
    Coroutine[DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]] CreditWalletAsync(
        str sellerId, str orderId, float amount,
        str tenantId)
    Coroutine[DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]] SchedulePayoutAsync(
        str sellerId, str orderId,
        str tenantId)
    # Triggered by CF-262 (dispute.opened) — payout.held via F579 outbox
    Coroutine[DataProcessResult[bool]] HoldPayoutAsync(
        str sellerId, str orderId, str disputeId,
        str tenantId)
    # Triggered by EP-2 timer (no dispute) OR dispute.decided (CF-265 cleared)
    # DNA-9: idempotencyKey required
    Coroutine[DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]] ReleasePayoutAsync(
        str sellerId, str orderId, str idempotencyKey,
        str tenantId)
    Coroutine[DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]] GetPayoutStatusAsync(
        str sellerId, str tenantId)
}
```

**DNA Compliance**: DNA-1 through DNA-9; DNA-8 (payout.held, payout.released via F579 outbox)

**PROTECTION WINDOW**: `marketplace.payout.protection_window_days` (FREEDOM config, default: 15)
EP-2 timer registered on delivery confirmation. CF-262 can override EP-2 release before window.

**Purpose**: Elasticsearch-backed marketplace discovery with mandatory PUBLISHED status filter

**FACTORY RESOLUTION**: F576 → DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch — listing search index, primary)
+ RAG FABRIC (IRagService.SearchAsync — social signals, read-only, CF-268)

**Interface**:

```python
class IMarketplaceSearchService(ABC):
{

    # socialContext OPTIONAL — sourced via IRagService only, never F234 direct (CF-268)

        dict[str, Any] searchParams,
        dict[str, Any]? socialContext,
        str tenantId)

        str listingId, dict[str, Any] listingDoc,
        str tenantId)
    # Called on listing.suspended / listing.deactivated
    Coroutine[DataProcessResult[bool]] DeIndexListingAsync(
        str listingId, str tenantId)
}
```

**DNA Compliance**: DNA-1 through DNA-6, DNA-2 (build_search_filter on all ES queries)

**Critical**: AF-7 validates socialContext sourcing — zero F234 factory method calls in
generated T226 code. Social signals accessed via IRagService.SearchAsync only (CF-268).

## F577 — IPromotionsRulesEngine

**Purpose**: Applies promotion rules (coupons, flash deals, volume discounts) to cart items and
search result overlays. Reads promotion definitions from Redis cache + Elasticsearch. All

**FACTORY RESOLUTION**: F577 → DATABASE FABRIC (Redis — active promotions cache, fast reads)
+ DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch — promotion rules index) + DATABASE FABRIC (PostgreSQL —
coupon redemption records)

**Interface**:
```python
class IPromotionsRulesEngine(ABC):
{
    # Cart-time coupon validation — checks eligibility, usage limits, expiry
    Coroutine[DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]] ValidateCouponAsync(
        str couponCode, str buyerId, str listingId,
        str tenantId)
    # Post-search overlay — injects flash deal badges, sponsored markers
    Coroutine[DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any][]]> ApplyPromotionOverlaysAsync(
        dict[str, Any][] searchResults,

}
```

can carry sponsored results), `marketplace.promotions.max_coupon_stack` (stackability rules)

## F578 — IIdempotencyKeyRegistry (EP-4 Factory Interface)

*Full specification above under EP-4.*

**FACTORY RESOLUTION**: F578 → DATABASE FABRIC (Redis) with TTL-backed key expiry.

**Summary**: GetOrCreateAsync, MarkInProgressAsync, MarkCompletedAsync, MarkFailedAsync.
This factory IS the DNA-9 pattern implementation. All financial factories depend on it.

---

## F579 — ITransactionalOutboxRelay (EP-5 Factory Interface)

*Full specification above under EP-5.*

**FACTORY RESOLUTION**: F579 → DATABASE FABRIC (PostgreSQL — outbox table) +
QUEUE FABRIC (Redis Streams — relay target).

**Summary**: WriteOutboxEventAsync (called within PG transaction by business factories),
RelayPendingEventsAsync (called by background relay worker only).

---

# DESIGN RECORDS — DR-83 through DR-88

## DR-83 — Saga Orchestrator Over Choreography for Financial Flows

**Decision**: T221, T222, T223, T224, T225 use orchestrator-centric saga coordination.

**Rationale**: Financial flows require deterministic compensation in LIFO order. Orchestrator
sagas provide: explicit step ordering, clear rollback path, debuggable execution trace,
idempotency enforcement point. Choreography's implicit coordination is too fragile for money
movement — a missing event handler means stuck funds.

**Rejected**: Pure choreography (harder to debug, implicit compensations, drift risk)
step-level hybrid (inconsistent failure semantics).

**Impact**: AF-2 (Planning) decomposes each financial task type into ordered steps + named
compensation steps. SK-126 (Saga Compensation Chain) is the canonical generated pattern.

---

## DR-84 — Bridge Tenancy as Default Marketplace Isolation Model

**Decision**: Pooled schema (shared PG tables + tenant_id column + DB-enforced RLS) as default.
Enterprise sellers (configurable tier) graduate to schema-per-tenant.

**Rationale**: Pooled: low cost, fast migrations, works for most SMB sellers.
Schema-per-tenant: stronger isolation blast radius, available via tier graduation.
Database-per-tenant: prohibitive ops cost at scale, reserved for future compliance-driven need.

**Rejected**: Schema-per-tenant as default (migration complexity without benefit for SMBs)
DB-per-tenant as default (ops cost); no isolation tiers (insufficient for enterprise).

**Impact**: CF-269 enforces isolation at application layer. All F566–F577 methods accept tenantId.
Tenant tier stored in `marketplace.tenant.isolation_tier` FREEDOM config.

---

## DR-85 — Idempotency Key TTL = 24h for Payment Operations

**Decision**: EP-4 TTL: financial ops = 24h, non-financial = 1h.

**Rationale**: Payment gateway retry windows are typically 6–12h. 24h provides 2× headroom
without unbounded Redis memory growth (keys auto-expire). Non-financial ops (cart lock, soft
inventory) have shorter natural TTLs so 1h is sufficient.

**Impact**: DNA-9 codifies TTL values as FREEDOM config defaults. F578.GetOrCreateAsync
accepts TTL as parameter — callers set TTL appropriate to their operation type.

---

## DR-86 — Marketplace UI Fabric Contract

**Decision**: All marketplace UI components generated as fabric-first with zero hardcoded
platform-specific values.

**What is configurable (FREEDOM layer)**:
- `marketplace.ui.payment_providers` — list of payment method names/logos
- `marketplace.ui.locale_config` — currency, date format, number format
- `marketplace.ui.checkout_steps_definition` — ordered DAG of checkout steps
- `marketplace.ui.search_facets` — which filters appear on search results
- `marketplace.ui.seller_dashboard_widgets` — seller dashboard composition

**Rationale**: Basic prompt requirement: "UI: Fabric-first, zero platform-specific values."
Marketplace UIs are typically the most platform-coupled layer; this design eliminates
that coupling so the engine can generate for any payment provider, any locale, any workflow.

**Impact**: DynamicController serves all UI config endpoints. DNA-6 enforced throughout.

---

## DR-87 — Transactional Outbox as Mandatory Pattern for Financial Events

**Decision**: All financial events (OrderPlaced, PaymentAuthorized, PaymentCaptured,
PaymentVoided, RefundExecuted, ShipmentCreated, PayoutReleased, PayoutHeld) MUST be written
via EP-5 (F579.WriteOutboxEventAsync) within the same DB transaction as the domain state change.

**Rationale**: The dual-write problem: if DB commits but queue publish fails, money moves
but fulfillment never starts. This is not recoverable without manual intervention. EP-5
guarantees atomic state+event commitment; the relay worker guarantees eventual publication.

**Rejected**: Direct IQueueService.EnqueueAsync after commit (dual-write risk)
saga-level retry (doesn't help if event was never published); deduplication downstream only
(shifts complexity to every consumer).

**Impact**: AF-7 Compliance validates: any financial factory calling IQueueService directly
(bypassing F579) = BUILD FAILURE. SK-128 is the canonical pattern.

---

## DR-88 — Two-Actor BFA Conflict Rules (Buyer vs Seller)

**Decision**: FLOW-16 introduces the first BFA rules that explicitly model cross-actor
conflicts between buyer and seller principals (CF-262, CF-265). Prior rules were within-actor
or within-system.

**Rationale**: Marketplace flows have two independent principals with competing interests:
buyer wants refund, seller wants payout. Without explicit cross-actor rules, the BFA cannot
detect scenarios like "dispute opens after payout already released" or "seller notified of
payout hold but not given reason." CF-262 and CF-265 are the first rules of this type.

**Impact**: BFA conflict rule vocabulary expanded to include `actorTypes` field on each rule.
CF-262: actorTypes = [buyer (opener), seller (blocked)]. CF-265: actorTypes = [system, seller].
AF-9 Judge validates cross-actor rule coverage for any task type touching both actor types.

### Family 75 — Identity, Profile & Reputation (F580–F586)

```
F580  IProfileService
      PURPOSE: Manage freelancer/client profiles, portfolio, skills, rates
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC (ES — profile discovery) + DATABASE FABRIC (PG — authoritative store)
      METHODS: CreateProfileAsync, UpdateProfileAsync, GetProfileAsync, SearchProfilesAsync
               → all return DataProcessResult[T]
      FREEDOM: Profile fields, searchable attributes, rate display settings
      DNA:     parse_document (all profile data as dict[str, Any])
               build_search_filter (empty field skip on profile search)
               DNA-5 Scope Isolation: tenantId + userId on every query

F581  IReputationService
      PURPOSE: Aggregate ratings, reviews, completion rates, badges
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC (ES — score queries) + DATABASE FABRIC (PG — immutable review log)
      METHODS: AddReviewAsync, ComputeScoreAsync, GetReputationSummaryAsync
      FREEDOM: Scoring weights, badge thresholds, minimum review counts
      DNA:     Immutable review append only (PG journal). Score derived, never mutated.
               tenantId isolation on all queries.

F582  ISkillTaxonomyService
      PURPOSE: Maintain skill categories, synonyms, AI-extracted skill normalisation
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC (ES) + AI ENGINE FABRIC (skill extraction prompts)
      METHODS: NormalizeSkillAsync, SearchSkillsAsync, ExtractSkillsFromTextAsync
      FREEDOM: Taxonomy configuration, AI extraction model selection, synonym maps

F583  IPortfolioService
      PURPOSE: Store and retrieve freelancer work samples, case studies, deliverable previews
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC (ES — portfolio search) + CORE FABRIC (object store ref)
      METHODS: AddPortfolioItemAsync, GetPortfolioAsync, SearchPortfolioAsync
      DNA:     File references only — never inline binary. tenantId scoped.

F584  IKYCService
      PURPOSE: Trigger, track and gate KYC verification (identity + anti-money-laundering)
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC (PG — KYC status records) + QUEUE FABRIC (KYC result events)
      METHODS: SubmitKYCAsync, GetKYCStatusAsync, ExpireKYCAsync
               → DataProcessResult[KycStatus] where KycStatus is dict[str, Any]
      FREEDOM: KYC provider routing (per tenant config), expiry window, required doc types
      IRON RULES: Contract CANNOT activate while KYC status ≠ VERIFIED

F585  ITokenWalletService
      PURPOSE: Manage proposal submission tokens ("Connects" pattern) — immutable ledger
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC (PG — immutable token ledger) + QUEUE FABRIC (spend events)
      METHODS: SpendTokensAsync(idempotencyKey), TopUpTokensAsync, GetBalanceAsync
               → all idempotency-key protected. DataProcessResult[T]
      FREEDOM: Token cost per proposal, boost token multiplier, tenant token grants
      IRON RULES: Cannot submit proposal with insufficient tokens. Idempotency key UNIQUE enforced at DB.

F586  IFreelancerTierService
      PURPOSE: Manage tier programs (Preferred Freelancer, Top-Rated, Rising Talent)
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC (ES — tier queries) + DATABASE FABRIC (PG — tier records)
      METHODS: EvaluateTierEligibilityAsync, PromoteTierAsync, GetTierStatusAsync
      FREEDOM: Tier criteria thresholds, perks per tier, evaluation schedule
```

```
F587  IJobService

F592  IJobCatalogService
```

### Family 77 — Proposal & Bidding Economy (F593–F598)

```
F593  IProposalService

F594  IProposalBoostService

      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC (PG — boost records) + F585 (token ledger) + F589 (ranking signals)
      METHODS: BoostProposalAsync(idempotencyKey), GetBoostRankAsync, ExpireBoostAsync
      FREEDOM: Max boost multiplier, boost duration window, auction competition rules
      IRON RULES: Boost requires active proposal (status = SUBMITTED). Token spend idempotent.

F595  IProposalRankingService
      PURPOSE: Compute and update display rank of proposals on a job, incorporating boosts + signals
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC (ES — ranking index) + AI ENGINE FABRIC (relevance scoring)
      METHODS: RankProposalsAsync, UpdateRankSignalAsync, GetRankedProposalListAsync
      FREEDOM: Ranking model (AI or rules-based), boost weight, recency decay factor

F596  IContestService

      METHODS: PostContestAsync, SubmitEntryAsync, AwardWinnerAsync, GetContestAsync
      FREEDOM: Contest duration, entry limits, guaranteed prize flag, anonymisation rules
      DNA:     Entry references deliverables via IDeliverableService (F613)

F597  IContestHandoverService
      PURPOSE: IP/ownership transfer gate — transfers contest entry ownership before prize release
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC (PG — handover records) + QUEUE FABRIC (ownership events)
      METHODS: InitiateHandoverAsync, CompleteHandoverAsync, GetHandoverStatusAsync
      IRON RULES: Prize CANNOT release until handover status = COMPLETED.
               Ownership transfer event emitted to QUEUE FABRIC before any prize payout.

F598  IConsultationService
      PURPOSE: Manage scheduled paid 1:1 expert sessions (availability, booking, follow-up)
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC (PG — booking records) + QUEUE FABRIC (booking events)
      METHODS: PublishAvailabilityAsync, BookSessionAsync, CompleteSessionAsync, CancelSessionAsync
      FREEDOM: Session duration options, per-minute vs per-session billing mode, follow-up rules
```

### Family 78 — Contract Management (F599–F604)

```
F599  IContractService
      PURPOSE: Contract creation, versioning, state transitions (OFFERED→ACTIVE→PAUSED→CLOSED)
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC (PG — contract store) + QUEUE FABRIC (contract events)
      METHODS: CreateContractAsync, ActivateContractAsync, PauseContractAsync, CloseContractAsync
      FREEDOM: Contract terms fields, billing type (HOURLY/MILESTONE/FIXED), enterprise governance flags
      DNA:     parse_document. tenantId + clientId + freelancerId on every contract document.
               DataProcessResult[T] — never throw for invalid state transitions.

F600  IContractVersioningService
      PURPOSE: Version control for contract definitions (support in-flight instance pinning)
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC (PG — version table) + DATABASE FABRIC (ES — version search)
      METHODS: CreateVersionAsync, GetVersionAsync, ListVersionsAsync, PinInstanceToVersionAsync
      FREEDOM: Max versions retained, auto-archive policy, version diff visibility

F601  IActivationGateService
      PURPOSE: Orchestrate all pre-activation checks (compliance, payment method, enterprise policy)
      FABRIC:  CORE FABRIC (gate orchestration) + F584 IKYCService + F603 ICompliancePolicyService
      METHODS: EvaluateActivationReadinessAsync, BlockActivationAsync, ClearActivationAsync
      IRON RULES: Returns DataProcessResult with gate status list.
               NEVER throws; blocked gates surface as IsSuccess=false with error metadata.

F602  IWorkerClassificationService
      PURPOSE: Assess misclassification risk for enterprise tenants before contract activation
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC (ES — policy rules) + AI ENGINE FABRIC (risk assessment)
      METHODS: AssessClassificationRiskAsync, GetClassificationStatusAsync, OverrideClassificationAsync
      FREEDOM: Risk threshold (per enterprise tenant config), AI model selection, override policy

F603  ICompliancePolicyService
      PURPOSE: Evaluate and enforce enterprise compliance policies (per tenant, per jurisdiction)
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC (ES — policy documents) + DATABASE FABRIC (PG — policy state)
      METHODS: EvaluatePolicyAsync, RegisterPolicyAsync, GetPolicyStatusAsync
      FREEDOM: Policy rules per tenant (FREEDOM config in ES), jurisdiction overrides, hard-stop list

F604  IEnterpriseGovernanceService
      PURPOSE: Enterprise program management: vendor approved lists, reporting, procurement governance
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC (ES — governance data) + QUEUE FABRIC (audit events)
      METHODS: CheckApprovedVendorListAsync, RecordGovernanceEventAsync, GenerateReportAsync
      DNA:     Append-only audit log. Every action = DataProcessResult. tenantId scoped.
```

### Family 79 — Milestone Escrow & Payments (F605–F610)

```
F605  IMilestoneService
      PURPOSE: Milestone lifecycle (CREATED→FUNDED→SUBMITTED→IN_REVIEW→RELEASED/REFUNDED/DISPUTED)
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC (PG — milestone records) + QUEUE FABRIC (milestone events)
      METHODS: CreateMilestoneAsync, SubmitDeliverableAsync, StartReviewAsync, ApproveMilestoneAsync
               → all return DataProcessResult[T]
      FREEDOM: Auto-release window (configurable per tenant), review period duration
      DNA:     parse_document. tenantId + contractId on every record.

F606  IEscrowService
      PURPOSE: Hold, release and refund funds — the escrow state machine
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC (PG — escrow state) + F607 IEscrowLedgerService
      METHODS: HoldFundsAsync(idempotencyKey), ReleaseFundsAsync(idempotencyKey),
               RefundFundsAsync(idempotencyKey), GetEscrowStatusAsync
      IRON RULES:
        IR-240-1: Cannot release if dispute is OPEN (CF-275)
        IR-240-2: Cannot refund after release
        IR-240-3: Cannot hold if insufficient verified payment method
        IR-240-4: All money operations MUST carry idempotency key
      DNA:     Idempotency key = UNIQUE constraint at DB level. Double-write prevention via outbox.

F607  IEscrowLedgerService
      PURPOSE: Immutable float-entry ledger for all escrow movements (FUND/RELEASE/REFUND/FEE/PAYOUT)
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC (PG — append-only journal)
      METHODS: JournalEntryAsync(idempotencyKey, type, amount, currency)
               GetLedgerAsync(contractId), ReconcileAsync
      IRON RULES: Journal is APPEND ONLY — no UPDATE or DELETE ever. Enforced at DB (no DELETE grant).
      DNA:     DataProcessResult[T]. tenantId scoped.

F608  IPayoutService
      PURPOSE: Schedule and execute payouts to freelancers after escrow release
      FABRIC:  QUEUE FABRIC (payout commands) + DATABASE FABRIC (PG — payout records)
      METHODS: SchedulePayoutAsync(idempotencyKey), ProcessPayoutAsync, GetPayoutStatusAsync
      FREEDOM: Payout batching window, security hold period (configurable per tenant), currency
      IRON RULES: Payout only after escrow RELEASED status confirmed. Security hold respected.

F609  IPaymentProtectionService
      PURPOSE: Evaluate payment protection eligibility (hourly: work diary evidence; fixed: escrow)
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC (ES — evidence queries) + F611 IWorkDiaryService
      METHODS: EvaluateHourlyProtectionAsync, EvaluateFixedProtectionAsync, GetProtectionStatusAsync
      FREEDOM: Evidence window, min activity threshold for hourly protection, dispute filing window

F610  IFeeCalculatorService
      PURPOSE: Compute platform fees, service fees, VAT/tax per transaction and per tenant plan
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC (PG — fee config) + CORE FABRIC
      METHODS: CalculateFeeAsync, GetFeeScheduleAsync
      FREEDOM: Fee percentages per plan tier, volume discounts, enterprise flat-fee overrides
      DNA:     Fee calculation is pure computation via DataProcessResult — never throws.
```

```
F611  IWorkDiaryService
```

### Family 81 — Dispute & Resolution (F616–F620)

```
F616  IDisputeService
      PURPOSE: Manage dispute lifecycle (OPEN→EVIDENCE→IN_REVIEW→RESOLVED→CLOSED)
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC (PG) + QUEUE FABRIC (dispute events → triggers escrow hold)
      METHODS: OpenDisputeAsync, CloseDisputeAsync, GetDisputeAsync, UpdateDisputeStatusAsync
      IRON RULES:
        IR-502-1: Opening dispute MUST trigger F617.HoldEscrowAsync immediately (via QUEUE FABRIC)
        IR-502-2: Dispute cannot close without resolution type set (TO_FREELANCER/TO_CLIENT/SPLIT)
      DNA:     parse_document. tenantId + contractId + milestoneId scoped.

F617  IDisputeLedgerHoldService
      PURPOSE: Place and lift escrow hold during dispute — integrates with F606/F607
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC (PG — hold records) + F606 IEscrowService
      METHODS: PlaceHoldAsync, LiftHoldAsync, GetHoldStatusAsync
      IRON RULES: Release/Refund on F606 BLOCKED while hold is ACTIVE (CF-275)

F618  IDisputeEvidenceService
      PURPOSE: Collect, store and retrieve evidence submissions during dispute
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC (ES — evidence metadata) + CORE FABRIC (object store ref)
      METHODS: SubmitEvidenceAsync, GetEvidenceAsync, ListEvidenceAsync
      FREEDOM: Evidence submission window, max evidence items, accepted file types
      DNA:     Evidence is immutable after submission. Object refs only.

F619  IArbitrationService
      PURPOSE: Assign, manage and track arbitration panel decision process
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC (PG — arbitration records) + AI ENGINE FABRIC (evidence summarisation)
      METHODS: AssignArbitratorAsync, RecordDecisionAsync, GetArbitrationStatusAsync
      FREEDOM: Arbitrator assignment rules (manual/AI-assisted), escalation SLA thresholds

F620  IResolutionService
      PURPOSE: Execute dispute resolution: release/refund/split escrow per decision
      FABRIC:  F606 IEscrowService + F607 IEscrowLedgerService + QUEUE FABRIC (resolution events)
      METHODS: ExecuteResolutionAsync(idempotencyKey, decision), GetResolutionStatusAsync
      IRON RULES: Resolution execution is idempotent (idempotency key). Records decision audit trail.
      DNA:     DataProcessResult[T]. Append-only audit entry after each resolution.
```

---

### Family 82 — Notifications & Communication (F621–F624)

```
F621  INotificationService
      PURPOSE: Send transactional notifications (proposal received, milestone funded, dispute opened…)
      FABRIC:  QUEUE FABRIC (notification commands) + CORE FABRIC (template resolution)
      METHODS: SendNotificationAsync, GetNotificationPreferencesAsync, UpdatePreferencesAsync
      FREEDOM: Per-tenant notification templates, channel priorities (email/push/webhook)

F622  IMessagingService
      PURPOSE: In-platform messaging between contract parties (proposals, Q&A, dispute evidence)
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC (ES — message search) + QUEUE FABRIC (message delivery)
      METHODS: SendMessageAsync, GetThreadAsync, SearchMessagesAsync
      DNA:     tenantId + threadId scoped. parse_document (no typed message model).

F623  IWebhookNotifyService
      PURPOSE: Push platform events to client-registered webhooks (enterprise integrations)
      FABRIC:  QUEUE FABRIC (outbox → webhook delivery) + DATABASE FABRIC (PG — webhook registry)
      METHODS: RegisterWebhookAsync, DeliverEventAsync(idempotencyKey), GetDeliveryStatusAsync
      IRON RULES: Delivery is idempotent. Retry with backoff. Max 5 attempts then DLQ.

F624  IAlertService
      PURPOSE: Generate system/operational alerts (rate limit breached, DLQ spike, compliance expiry)
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC (ES — alert index) + QUEUE FABRIC
      METHODS: RaiseAlertAsync, GetAlertsAsync, AcknowledgeAlertAsync
```

---

### Family 83 — Enterprise Compliance & Governance (F625–F630)

```
F625  ITenantComplianceService
      PURPOSE: Per-tenant compliance profile: KYC status, jurisdictions, compliance gates active
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC (PG — compliance profile) + F584 IKYCService
      METHODS: GetComplianceProfileAsync, SetComplianceGateAsync, EvaluateComplianceAsync
      FREEDOM: Per-tenant gate configuration (which checks are required by policy)
      DNA:     tenantId scoped on ALL records. Hard-stop gates stored as FREEDOM config in ES.

F626  IAuditLogService
      PURPOSE: Append-only audit trail for all state changes, money movements and access events
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC (PG WORM — write-once audit table) + DATABASE FABRIC (ES — audit search)
      METHODS: RecordEventAsync, SearchAuditAsync, ExportAuditAsync
      IRON RULES: Audit log is append-only forever. No UPDATE/DELETE ever. Enforced at DB level.
      DNA:     Every write = new immutable row. tenantId + actorId + action + target + timestamp.

F627  IEnterpriseReportService
      PURPOSE: Generate compliance, spend, and activity reports for enterprise program management
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC (ES — aggregation queries) + AI ENGINE FABRIC (report summaries)
      METHODS: GenerateSpendReportAsync, GenerateComplianceReportAsync, ScheduleReportAsync
      FREEDOM: Report formats, delivery schedules, aggregation granularity per tenant

F628  ICompliancePolicyEngineService
      PURPOSE: Evaluate complex multi-condition compliance policies (worker classification, GDPR, PCI)
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC (ES — policy rules) + AI ENGINE FABRIC (policy analysis)
      METHODS: EvaluatePolicyAsync, RegisterPolicyRuleAsync, GetPolicyEvaluationAsync
      FREEDOM: Policy rule sets (stored as FREEDOM config in ES, not hardcoded)

F629  IDataRetentionService
      PURPOSE: Manage data retention schedules (evidence, work diary, contracts) per jurisdiction
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC (PG — retention policy records) + QUEUE FABRIC (deletion jobs)
      METHODS: SetRetentionPolicyAsync, ProcessRetentionAsync, GetRetentionStatusAsync
      FREEDOM: Retention period per data type per jurisdiction — fully FREEDOM-configurable

F630  IIPTransferService
      PURPOSE: Record and certify intellectual property ownership transfer (contest handover, work product)
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC (PG — IP transfer records, immutable) + QUEUE FABRIC
      METHODS: RecordIPTransferAsync, CertifyTransferAsync, GetIPStatusAsync
      IRON RULES: IP transfer record is immutable after CERTIFIED status. Append-only.
      DNA:     tenantId + fromParty + toParty + workProductRef + timestamp. parse_document only.
```

---

## DNA COMPLIANCE SUMMARY — ALL F580–F630

| DNA Pattern | Enforcement in FLOW-17 |
|-------------|------------------------|
| DNA-1 parse_document | All 51 factories use dict[str, Any] — no typed models |
| DNA-2 build_search_filter | All ES queries use build_search_filter (F587, F589, F590, F595, F611, F627) |
| DNA-3 DataProcessResult[T] | All 51 factories return DataProcessResult[T] — never throw |
| DNA-4 MicroserviceBase | All generated services extend MicroserviceBase (19 components) |
| DNA-5 Scope Isolation | tenantId mandatory on every factory method, every document, every query |
| DNA-6 DynamicController | No entity-specific controllers generated — DynamicController only |

---

## KEY INVARIANTS — FLOW-17

| ID | Invariant | Enforcement |
|----|-----------|-------------|
| INV-17-1 | Escrow immutable journal — append only | DR-89, F607, IR-492-4 |
| INV-17-2 | Cannot release escrow while dispute OPEN | CF-275, F617, IR-492-1 |
| INV-17-3 | Token wallet spend is idempotent | DR-90, F585, F593 |
| INV-17-4 | KYC must be VERIFIED before contract activation | CF-278, F584, F601 |
| INV-17-5 | IP transfer must be CERTIFIED before prize release | DR-95, F597, F630 |
| INV-17-6 | Audit log is append-only forever | DR-91, F626 |
| INV-17-7 | Work diary screenshot = external ref only (never inline) | DR-93, F611 |
| INV-17-8 | All money ops carry idempotency key | DR-89, F606/F607/F608 |
| INV-17-9 | Deliverables immutable after submission | DR-94, F613 |
| INV-17-10 | tenantId on every factory method — zero exceptions | DNA-5, all F580-F630 |

## FABRIC INTERFACE REFERENCE (unchanged — all FLOW-18 factories resolve through these)

```
DATABASE FABRIC    (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) — IDatabaseService)  → ES, PG, Redis, Mongo, MySQL, SQL Server
QUEUE FABRIC       (SK-383 (RedisStreamQueue) — IQueueService)     → Redis Streams consumer groups
AI ENGINE FABRIC   (SK-385 (IAiProvider)/07 — IAiProvider + AiDispatcher) → Claude, OpenAI, Gemini, DeepSeek
RAG FABRIC         (SK-388 (IRagService)/00b — IRagService)   → 7 strategies, config-selected
CORE FABRIC        (SK-379 (MicroserviceBase) — MicroserviceBase)   → 19 inherited components
FLOW ENGINE FABRIC (SK-391 (FlowOrchestrator)/09 — IFlowDefinition + IFlowOrchestrator) → JSON DAG orchestration
```

## FACTORY REGISTRY — F631–F696

### FAMILY 84 — Flow Canvas (F631–F636)

```
F631: IFlowCanvasService
  PURPOSE: Visual flow canvas — node placement, edge creation, DAG layout management
  FABRIC RESOLUTION:
    PRIMARY → DATABASE FABRIC (ES) via IDatabaseService.StoreDocument/SearchDocuments
    SECONDARY → QUEUE FABRIC (Redis Streams) via IQueueService for canvas change events
  METHODS: CreateCanvasAsync, SaveCanvasStateAsync, LoadCanvasAsync,
           UndoAsync, RedoAsync, ExportDAGAsync — all return DataProcessResult[T]
  DNA COMPLIANCE: DNA-1, DNA-2, DNA-4, DNA-5, DNA-6
  FACTORY RESOLUTION: CreateAsync(FactoryResolutionContext{provider:"elasticsearch"})
  FREEDOM CONFIG: max nodes per canvas (default 200), auto-save interval (default 30s),
                  undo history depth (default 50), grid snap size

F632: IFlowNodeService
  PURPOSE: Node CRUD within a canvas — create, configure, connect, delete nodes
  FABRIC RESOLUTION:
    PRIMARY → DATABASE FABRIC (ES) via IDatabaseService
    SECONDARY → AI ENGINE FABRIC (SK-386 (AiDispatcher)) for node configuration suggestions

           ValidateNodeAsync, GetNodeSchemaAsync — all return DataProcessResult[T]
  DNA COMPLIANCE: DNA-1 (node config as Dictionary), DNA-2, DNA-4, DNA-5
  FACTORY RESOLUTION: CreateAsync(FactoryResolutionContext{provider:"elasticsearch"})
  FREEDOM CONFIG: allowed node types per tenant tier, max connections per node

F633: IFlowNodePaletteService

    SECONDARY → RAG FABRIC (SK-389 (HybridRagStrategy)) via IRagService.SearchAsync for skill discovery
  METHODS: GetPaletteAsync, SearchNodesAsync, GetNodeContractAsync,
           SuggestNodesAsync — all return DataProcessResult[T]
  DNA COMPLIANCE: DNA-1, DNA-2, DNA-4, DNA-5

F634: IFlowEdgeService
```

```
  METHODS: ExecuteFlowAsync, ExecuteStepAsync, PauseAsync, ResumeAsync,
           GetExecutionStateAsync — all return DataProcessResult[T]
  DNA COMPLIANCE: DNA-1, DNA-2, DNA-3, DNA-4, DNA-5
  FACTORY RESOLUTION: CreateAsync(FactoryResolutionContext{provider:"flow-orchestrator"})
  FREEDOM CONFIG: step timeout, breakpoint support, variable inspection

  PURPOSE: Define and validate assertions against sandbox execution results

    PRIMARY → CORE FABRIC (ObjectProcessor) for assertion evaluation
    SECONDARY → DATABASE FABRIC (ES) for assertion template storage

           SuggestAssertionsAsync — all return DataProcessResult[T]
  DNA COMPLIANCE: DNA-1, DNA-3, DNA-4
  FACTORY RESOLUTION: CreateAsync(FactoryResolutionContext{provider:"core"})
```

### FAMILY 88 — Fabric-First UI Layer (F660–F667)

```
F660: IUIComponentFabricService
  PURPOSE: Fabric interface for ALL UI components — resolves to platform renderer via config
  FABRIC RESOLUTION: PRIMARY → CORE FABRIC (SK-379 (MicroserviceBase))
  PLATFORM RESOLUTION (FREEDOM CONFIG):
    "ui.platform": "react"        → ReactRenderer
    "ui.platform": "angular"      → AngularRenderer
    "ui.platform": "svelte"       → SvelteRenderer
    "ui.platform": "vue"          → VueRenderer
    "ui.platform": "react-native" → NativeRenderer
  METHODS: RenderComponentAsync, HandleEventAsync, UpdateStateAsync,
           GetComponentRegistryAsync — all return DataProcessResult[T]
  DNA COMPLIANCE: DNA-1, DNA-4, DNA-6
  CRITICAL: ALL UI code goes through this fabric — never import React/Angular directly
```

---

## DESIGN RECORDS — FLOW-18 (DR-99 through DR-110)

| DR | Decision | Rationale |
|----|----------|-----------|
| DR-99 | Canvas state stored in ES (not PG) | Real-time search + complex nested DAG structures favor document store |
| DR-100 | Node palette backed by RAG search | 63+ existing skills + dynamic skill growth → RAG discovery essential |
| DR-101 | Edge conditions use JSON Logic | More portable than CEL; parseable without runtime; safe in sandbox |
| DR-102 | Generated code always extends MicroserviceBase | DNA-4 non-negotiable; ensures 19 inherited components on all generated services |
| DR-103 | Sandbox environments ephemeral (TTL) | Prevents resource leak; bounded cost; deterministic cleanup |
| DR-104 | Iron rule validation before factory registration | Reject non-compliant code before it enters the factory registry |
| DR-105 | UI fabric resolves via config, never import | Allows platform swap without regenerating any flow designer logic |
| DR-106 | All UI endpoints via DynamicController (DNA-6) | No entity-specific controllers for canvas, palette, toolbar, etc. |
| DR-107 | Collaboration uses CRDT (not OT) for convergence | CRDTs handle disconnected editing better; eventual consistency guaranteed |
| DR-108 | Marketplace flows require sandbox pass before listing | Quality gate prevents broken flows from entering marketplace |
| DR-109 | Promotion requires feature flag (no direct deploy) | Canary rollout protects production from untested generated code |
| DR-110 | External git sync via HTTP (no SDKs) | Fabric-first: no Octokit/GitLab SDK import; generic HTTP only |

---

## DNA COMPLIANCE — FLOW-18

| DNA Pattern | Enforcement in FLOW-18 |
|-------------|----------------------|
| DNA-1 parse_document | All 66 factories — canvas state, node config, flow defs all dict[str, Any] |
| DNA-2 build_search_filter | F631, F633, F645, F650, F671, F674, F688 — skip empty fields |
| DNA-3 DataProcessResult[T] | All T247-T268 iron rules enforce no-throw pattern |
| DNA-4 MicroserviceBase | All 66 factory implementations extend MicroserviceBase |
| DNA-5 Scope Isolation | tenantId on every canvas, sandbox, marketplace, promotion operation |
| DNA-6 DynamicController | All UI endpoints (F660-F667) via DynamicController — no entity-specific controllers |
| DNA-7 Idempotency-First | F646 injection, F681 promotion — idempotency keys on all writes |
| DNA-8 Outbox Pattern | F646, F681 — injection/promotion events via transactional outbox |
| DNA-9 Extended | F643 code generation — idempotency on generation attempts |

---

## KEY INVARIANTS — FLOW-18

| ID | Invariant | Enforcement |
|----|-----------|-------------|
| INV-18-1 | Generated code MUST pass all 9 DNA patterns before injection | F644, CF-302 |
| INV-18-2 | No factory registration without fabric resolution mapping | F645, CF-305 |
| INV-18-3 | Sandbox never accesses production data | F652, CF-312 |
| INV-18-4 | Promotion requires sandbox pass + human approval | F681, F684, CF-315 |
| INV-18-5 | UI components never import platform modules | F660, DR-105 |
| INV-18-6 | External git sync via HTTP only (no SDKs) | F692, DR-110 |
| INV-18-7 | Canvas state persisted atomically | F631, IR-247-1 |
| INV-18-8 | All marketplace flows must pass quality gates | F674, DR-108, CF-318 |
