```
               or explicit location (profile). Radius targeting with configurable precision
               (FREEDOM config — coarser radius for privacy compliance).
      METHODS: IsInTargetAsync(viewerLocation, locationTarget, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[bool]
               ResolveViewerLocationAsync(viewerContext, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]

F792  IDemographicTargetService
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC → PostgreSQL (demographic profile)
      PURPOSE: Evaluates demographic targeting: age range, gender, language, education,
               employment. All demographic targeting subject to consent gate (F793)
               and minor protection (F752). Demographic data from user profile, not
               inferred — inference is treated as a sensitive topic requiring consent.
      METHODS: EvaluateDemographicAsync(viewerId, target, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[bool]

F793  IConsentLookupService
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC → Redis (consent cache, fast-path) + PG (authoritative)
      PURPOSE: The consent gate. Called BEFORE any targeting evaluation. Returns
               effective consent status for a viewer for a given targeting context
               (personalised ads, interest targeting, location targeting). If consent
               absent, targeting evaluation is SKIPPED entirely (not filtered post-eval).
               (DD-167, DR-128)
      METHODS: GetConsentAsync(viewerId, consentType, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]
               HasConsentAsync(viewerId, consentType, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[bool]
      DNA:     Scope Isolation — consent always tenantId-scoped
               DataProcessResult[T] — consent lookup failure → conservative deny-result

F794  IExclusionListService
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC → Elasticsearch (exclusion segment index)
      PURPOSE: Manages audience exclusions at campaign and ad-set level: exclude existing
               customers, exclude converters, competitor exclusions. Exclusion check is
               applied AFTER consent gate and AFTER audience inclusion — exclusion has
               higher priority than inclusion.
      METHODS: IsExcludedAsync(viewerId, adSetId, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[bool]
               AddExclusionAsync(adSetId, segmentId, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[bool]
```

---

# FAMILY 114 — Auction & Delivery
## F795–F811 | Scope: Eligibility, quality score, pricing, frequency cap, pacing, insertion

Auction engine (F810) is STATELESS. All mutable state in Redis only. <50ms p99. (DD-166)

```
F795  IEligibleAdsLoaderService
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC → ES (ad catalog) + Redis (eligibility cache)
      PURPOSE: Retrieves the candidate set of eligible ads for a given ad slot and
               viewer context. Pre-filters by: slot placement, review approval status
               (F786 "approved" only — DD-178), targeting match (F787), budget
               availability (F774), flight dates (F772). Returns ordered candidate list.
      METHODS: LoadEligibleAdsAsync(slotContext, viewerContext, tenantId) → DataProcessResult<list[object]>
               IsEligibleAsync(adId, slotContext, viewerContext, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[bool]

F796  IQualityScoreService
      FABRIC:  AI ENGINE FABRIC (relevance scoring) + DATABASE FABRIC → ES (score cache)
      PURPOSE: Computes quality score for each candidate ad in the auction. Combines:
               predicted click-through rate (pCTR), creative quality score (F778),
               ad relevance to viewer context (F790), and historical performance.
               AF-5 multi-model scoring; conservative on divergence > 10% (DD-175).
      METHODS: ScoreAsync(adId, viewerContext, slotContext, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]

F797  IPricingModelService
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC → Elasticsearch (pricing model FREEDOM config)
      PURPOSE: Applies the pricing model for ranking: eCPM = bid × quality score for
               CPM, eCPC = bid × pCTR × quality for CPC. Pricing model variant
               (GSP / first-price / hybrid) is FREEDOM config per placement type.
               Generalised Second Price (GSP) is the default for most placements.
      METHODS: ComputeRankScoreAsync(ad, pricingContext, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]
               GetPricingModelAsync(placementType, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]

F798  IFrequencyCapService
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC → Redis (INCR atomic counters, TTL-managed)
      PURPOSE: Enforces frequency caps per viewer per ad/campaign/ad-set over rolling
               windows (1h/24h/7d). Redis INCR is atomic and sub-millisecond.
               Counter key: tenantId:viewerId:entityId:window. TTL = window duration.
               Cap values are FREEDOM config per campaign.
      METHODS: CheckFrequencyAsync(viewerId, entityId, window, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[bool]
               IncrementAsync(viewerId, entityId, window, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[int]

F799  IPacingService
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC → Redis (pacing state, sub-millisecond read/write)
      PURPOSE: Manages pacing of ad spend against time budget. Even pacing: spend ~1/N
               of daily budget in each 1/N time slice. Pacing state (target_rate,
               current_rate, adjustment_factor) read from Redis in auction path.
               Spend-too-fast → throttle; spend-too-slow → boost. State updated via
               async QUEUE FABRIC event after each impression. (DD-166)
      METHODS: GetPacingSignalAsync(adSetId, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]
               UpdatePacingStateAsync(adSetId, impressionEvent, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[bool]

F800  IAdSlotResolverService
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC → Elasticsearch (slot definition FREEDOM config)
      PURPOSE: Resolves ad slot specifications for a given placement context: slot size,
               allowed creative formats, frequency cap defaults, max ads per page.
               Slot definitions are FREEDOM config — product team can add/modify slots
               without code deployment. Feeds F795 (eligibility) and F804 (insertion).
      METHODS: ResolveSlotAsync(placementContext, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]
               ListSlotsAsync(surface, tenantId) → DataProcessResult<list[object]>

F801  IAdCatalogService
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC → Elasticsearch (ad index with approval status)
      PURPOSE: The ad catalog is the indexed store of all ads with their current
               approval state, targeting, bid, quality scores, and creative refs.
               F809 EligibilityChecker queries this catalog as the first filter.
               Catalog updates are event-driven: review decisions (F786), budget
               changes (F774), and status changes (F771/F772) each trigger catalog
               update via QUEUE FABRIC.
      METHODS: GetAdAsync(adId, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]
               SearchAdsAsync(filters, tenantId) → DataProcessResult<list[object]>
               UpdateApprovalStatusAsync(adId, status, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[bool]

F802  IAdReviewService
      FABRIC:  AI ENGINE FABRIC (automated review classifier) + DATABASE FABRIC → ES
      PURPOSE: Orchestrates the ad review pipeline: (1) auto-classify via AI ENGINE
               FABRIC, (2) check F782 content policy, (3) detect political content
               via F803 + F806 dual-gate (DD-168), (4) queue for human review if
               auto-review insufficient, (5) record decision in F786. "Approved"
               status recorded in F801 catalog. (DD-178)
      METHODS: SubmitForReviewAsync(adId, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[str] (reviewId)
               GetReviewStatusAsync(adId, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]

F803  IContentClassifierService
      FABRIC:  AI ENGINE FABRIC (multi-class content classifier)
      PURPOSE: AI-powered content classifier for ad creative: detects political content,
               violence, adult content, hate speech, deceptive claims. Returns confidence
               scores per class. For political detection, classifier result is ONLY the
               first gate — explicit verification via F806 is always required. (DD-168)
      METHODS: ClassifyAsync(assetId, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]
               GetClassificationScoresAsync(assetId, tenantId) → DataProcessResult<list[object]>

F804  ISponsoredInsertionService
      FABRIC:  QUEUE FABRIC → Redis Streams (feed injection event stream)
      PURPOSE: Inserts the auction winner into the feed/search results/placement surface.
               Emits insertion event to QUEUE FABRIC — downstream feed assembly service
               consumes and injects ad at designated slot position. Insertion is async
               fallback (no ad) if consumer timeout exceeded. Impression logged via F805.
      METHODS: InsertSponsoredAdAsync(auctionResult, slotContext, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[str]
               GetInsertionStatusAsync(insertionId, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]

F805  IImpressionLogService
      FABRIC:  QUEUE FABRIC → Redis Streams (impression event stream, high throughput)
      PURPOSE: Logs ad impressions to QUEUE FABRIC for downstream processing: attribution
               (F818), billing trigger (F832), frequency cap increment (F798), pacing
               update (F799). High-throughput write — impression events never block the
               render path. Consumer acknowledges after processing.
      METHODS: LogImpressionAsync(impressionEvent, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[str]

F806  IPoliticalVerificationService
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC → PostgreSQL (political verification records)
      PURPOSE: Explicit political advertiser verification. Required for any ad classified
               as political by F803, regardless of confidence score. Stores verification
               status, legal entity name, jurisdiction, and authorising officer. Verification
               is required per jurisdiction — US/EU/UK have different requirements.
               (DD-168, DR-129)
      METHODS: GetVerificationStatusAsync(advertiserId, jurisdiction, tenantId)
                 → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]
               RecordVerificationAsync(advertiserId, verificationData, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[str]

F807  IBrandSafetyService
      FABRIC:  AI ENGINE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC → ES (brand safety rules)
      PURPOSE: Evaluates brand safety for ad placement: is the surrounding content context
               appropriate for this ad? Checks content category of the page/post against
               advertiser brand safety settings (FREEDOM config). Advertisers can set
               blocking categories (e.g., "do not appear next to news about violence").
               Minor protection (F752) feeds into brand safety for youth-targeted placements.
      METHODS: EvaluateBrandSafetyAsync(adId, contentContext, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]
               GetBlockingCategoriesAsync(adId, tenantId) → DataProcessResult<list[object]>

F808  IPlacementAuditService
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC → Elasticsearch (placement audit log, append-only)
      PURPOSE: Append-only audit log of every ad placement decision: auction winner,
               runner-up prices, quality scores, targeting match factors, brand safety
               decision. Used for: advertiser reporting, regulatory audit, dispute
               resolution. Consistent with DD-169 append-only principle.
      METHODS: LogPlacementAsync(placementDecision, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[str]
               GetPlacementAuditAsync(adId, dateRange, tenantId) → DataProcessResult<list[object]>

F809  IEligibilityCheckerService
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC → ES (ad catalog) + Redis (eligibility cache, TTL 5min)
      PURPOSE: Hard eligibility filter. Checks before any auction calculation:
               (1) Creative approval status = "approved" (DD-178) — hard block if not
               (2) Budget available (F774)
               (3) Flight dates active (F772)
               (4) Account not suspended (F766)
               (5) Political verification where required (F806)
               (6) Not on exclusion list (F794)
               Cached per ad per slot for 5 min to avoid repeated ES reads in hot path.
      METHODS: CheckEligibilityAsync(adId, slotContext, viewerContext, tenantId)
                 → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]

F810  IAuctionEngineService
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC → Redis ONLY (stateless, all mutable state in Redis)
      PURPOSE: Pure stateless auction function. Takes eligible candidate ads from F795,
               quality scores from F796, pricing from F797. Runs eCPM ranking.
               Applies pacing signal from F799 (Redis). Applies frequency cap from F798
               (Redis). Winner determined. All state reads: Redis ONLY. No PG call in
               auction path. <50ms p99 SLO. (DD-166, DR-127)
      METHODS: RunAuctionAsync(candidates, slotContext, viewerContext, tenantId)
                 → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]
      DNA:     DATABASE FABRIC → Redis ONLY in auction critical path
               DataProcessResult[T] — no-fill (empty auction) is a valid result

F811  IBudgetDecrementService
      FABRIC:  QUEUE FABRIC (async event consumer) + DATABASE FABRIC → Redis (INCR)
      PURPOSE: Consumes billing events from QUEUE FABRIC and atomically decrements
               the Redis spend counter for the campaign/ad set. Never in the auction
               critical path — decrement is async after impression log. If Redis
               counter reaches budget limit, emits QUEUE FABRIC event → F774 to pause
               campaign. (DD-166 — decrement decoupled from auction)
      METHODS: ProcessBillingEventAsync(billingEvent, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[bool]
               GetCurrentSpendAsync(entityId, entityType, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]
```

---

# FAMILY 115 — Measurement & Attribution
## F812–F821 | Scope: Impression/click tracking, conversion, attribution engine

```
F812  IImpressionTrackerService
      FABRIC:  QUEUE FABRIC → Redis Streams (impression stream) + DATABASE FABRIC → ES
      PURPOSE: Processes raw impression events from F805. Enriches with viewability
               signals (F817), fraud signals (F822–F824), and device context.
               Emits enriched impression event to attribution engine (F818) after
               fraud gate (F823 — blocking, DD-177).
      METHODS: ProcessImpressionAsync(impressionEvent, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]

F813  IClickTrackerService
      FABRIC:  QUEUE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC → ES (click log)
      PURPOSE: Processes click events: validates click is on a served impression (anti-
               fraud correlation), deduplicates (F765 pattern applied to clicks),
               enriches with device and context, routes to F818 attribution engine
               after fraud gate (F823 — blocking, DD-177).
      METHODS: ProcessClickAsync(clickEvent, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]

F814  IConversionPixelService
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC → PostgreSQL (pixel registry)
      PURPOSE: Manages web conversion pixel (JavaScript snippet) registrations: pixel ID,
               event types (page_view, add_to_cart, purchase, lead), value mapping,
               and deduplication window. Pixel fires POST to the server event endpoint
               (F815) for each conversion event.
      METHODS: CreatePixelAsync(spec, accountId, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[str]
               GetPixelAsync(pixelId, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]
               ValidatePixelFireAsync(pixelId, eventData, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[bool]

F815  IServerConversionService
      FABRIC:  QUEUE FABRIC → Redis Streams (server event ingestion stream)
      PURPOSE: Server-side conversion API endpoint: accepts POST from advertiser server
               with conversion event (purchase, lead, subscription). Validates signature,
               deduplicates (by advertiser-provided event_id), routes to F818 attribution.
               Server events have higher attribution weight than pixel events (less lossy).
      METHODS: IngestServerEventAsync(serverEvent, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[str]
               DeduplicateEventAsync(eventId, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[bool]

F816  IAppEventService
      FABRIC:  QUEUE FABRIC (mobile event stream)
      PURPOSE: Ingests mobile app events from platform SDK: app_install, app_open,
               in_app_purchase, level_complete, subscription. Validates SDK token,
               routes to F818 attribution engine. App events are deduplicated by
               install_id + event_name + timestamp window.
      METHODS: IngestAppEventAsync(appEvent, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[str]

F817  IViewabilityService
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC → Elasticsearch (viewability log)
      PURPOSE: Records viewability signals from client-side measurement: percent in
               viewport, time-in-view, MRC viewability standard (50% pixels for 1 second
               continuous for display). Viewability data feeds quality reporting (F834)
               and quality score refresh (F796). Viewability below threshold triggers
               "not viewable" flag — not billed for some pricing models.
      METHODS: RecordViewabilityAsync(viewabilityEvent, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[bool]
               GetViewabilityStatsAsync(adId, dateRange, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]

F818  IAttributionEngineService
      FABRIC:  AI ENGINE FABRIC (attribution model scoring) + DATABASE FABRIC → PG
      PURPOSE: Assigns conversion credit to ad touchpoints. Applies attribution model
               from F820 (last-click / first-click / linear / data-driven). Respects
               attribution window from F829 (FREEDOM config per advertiser — DD-179).
               Cross-device attribution via F819. Fraud-screened events only (DD-177).
      METHODS: AttributeConversionAsync(conversionEvent, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]
               GetAttributionPathAsync(conversionId, tenantId) → DataProcessResult<list[object]>

F819  ICrossDeviceService
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC → PostgreSQL (identity graph)
      PURPOSE: Resolves cross-device identity: same user on mobile + desktop + tablet.
               Identity graph built from deterministic signals (login) and probabilistic
               (device graph, IP household). Required for accurate cross-device attribution
               and frequency cap enforcement across devices.
      METHODS: ResolveDeviceClusterAsync(deviceId, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]
               GetLinkedDevicesAsync(userId, tenantId) → DataProcessResult<list[object]>

F820  IAttributionModelService
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC → Elasticsearch (model definition FREEDOM config)
      PURPOSE: Stores and resolves attribution model definitions per advertiser account:
               last-click, first-click, linear (equal weight), time-decay, position-based,
               and data-driven (AI-powered based on historical conversion data). Model
               selection is FREEDOM config per account.
      METHODS: GetModelAsync(accountId, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]
               SetModelAsync(accountId, model, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[bool]

F821  IAttributionWindowService
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC → Elasticsearch (window config FREEDOM config)
      PURPOSE: Resolves click-through and view-through attribution windows per advertiser
               account. Default: 7d click, 1d view; customisable per account (DD-179).
               Window config is FREEDOM config — advertiser can adjust for their
               conversion cycle without platform code change.
      METHODS: GetWindowAsync(accountId, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]
               SetWindowAsync(accountId, clickWindow, viewWindow, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[bool]
```

---

# FAMILY 116 — Fraud Detection & Revenue Integrity
## F822–F829 | Scope: Invalid traffic, click fraud, bot detection, fraud gate

Fraud gate (F823/F831) is BLOCKING before billing event emission. (DD-177)

```
F822  IInvalidTrafficService
      FABRIC:  AI ENGINE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC → ES (IVT log)
      PURPOSE: General-purpose invalid traffic (IVT) detector: covers both general
               invalid traffic (GIVT: bots, crawlers, data centres) and sophisticated
               invalid traffic (SIVT: spoofed user agents, falsified referrers). Uses
               AI ENGINE FABRIC for pattern analysis; Redis cache for known-bad signatures.
      METHODS: EvaluateTrafficAsync(event, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]
               GetIVTSignalsAsync(event) → DataProcessResult<list[object]>

F823  IClickFraudService
      FABRIC:  AI ENGINE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC → ES (fraud signals)
      PURPOSE: Click fraud scoring: velocity-based (same user clicking same ad repeatedly),
               coordinate-based (geographic clustering of clicks), and behavioural
               (no dwell time after click, no scroll). Fraud score above threshold →
               quarantine event (F828) BEFORE billing emission. (DD-177)
      METHODS: ScoreClickAsync(clickEvent, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]
               IsFraudulentAsync(clickEvent, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[bool]

F824  IBotDetectionService
      FABRIC:  AI ENGINE FABRIC (bot fingerprint classifier) + DATABASE FABRIC → Redis
      PURPOSE: Detects bot traffic from: headless browser signals, abnormal interaction
               patterns, missing browser APIs, mouse movement entropy, touch event
               analysis. Bot confidence score; above threshold → quarantine (F828).
      METHODS: ScoreBotLikelihoodAsync(interactionContext, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]

F825  IIPReputationService
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC → Redis (IP reputation cache) + ES (reputation index)
      PURPOSE: Evaluates IP address reputation: data-centre IP, known proxy/VPN,
               Tor exit node, known-bad actor list. Used by F823 (fraud signal) and
               F791 (location targeting enrichment). Reputation data updated via
               external threat feed integration (QUEUE FABRIC consumer).
      METHODS: GetIPReputationAsync(ipAddress, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]
               IsDataCentreIPAsync(ipAddress) → DataProcessResult[bool]

F826  IDeviceFingerprintService
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC → Redis (fingerprint store)
      PURPOSE: Stores and matches device fingerprints: canvas fingerprint, WebGL hash,
               font list, screen resolution, timezone. Used for cross-device linking
               (F819 probabilistic) and fraud detection (repeated visits from same
               fingerprint with different claimed user IDs).
      METHODS: StoreFingerprint(fingerprint, deviceId, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[bool]
               MatchFingerprintAsync(fingerprint, tenantId) → DataProcessResult<dict[str, Any]?>

F827  IBehaviorAnomalyService
      FABRIC:  AI ENGINE FABRIC (anomaly detection model) + DATABASE FABRIC → ES
      PURPOSE: Detects behavioural anomalies in ad interaction sequences: unnatural
               dwell times, programmatic scrolling patterns, click-then-immediate-back,
               and coordinated amplification patterns (same content clicked by group
               at suspiciously similar intervals). Feeds F823 and F828.
      METHODS: AnalyzeSequenceAsync(interactionSequence, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]

F828  IFraudQuarantineService
      FABRIC:  QUEUE FABRIC → DLQ (fraud quarantine queue) + DATABASE FABRIC → ES (audit)
      PURPOSE: Quarantines fraudulent events synchronously before billing. Quarantined
               events sent to DLQ for human review and reporting to IAB TechLab fraud
               reporting pipeline. Quarantine is blocking — billing event never emitted
               for quarantined impression/click. (DD-177)
      METHODS: QuarantineEventAsync(event, fraudSignals, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[str]
               GetQuarantineAuditAsync(dateRange, tenantId) → DataProcessResult<list[object]>

F829  IAttributionConfigService
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC → Elasticsearch (attribution config FREEDOM config)
      PURPOSE: Stores and resolves attribution window configuration per advertiser account.
               Separate from F821 (which reads the window) — this service owns the
               FREEDOM config document: {clickWindow_days, viewWindow_days, attributionModel,
               crossDeviceEnabled}. Admin or advertiser can update via dashboard.
               (DD-179, DR-133)
      METHODS: GetConfigAsync(accountId, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]
               UpdateConfigAsync(accountId, config, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[bool]
```

---

# FAMILY 117 — Billing, Reporting & Finance
## F830–F837 | Scope: Spend ledger, billing events, invoicing, reporting, forecasting

All spend ledger entries are APPEND-ONLY. No UPDATE or DELETE on billing records. (DD-169)

```
F830  ISpendLedgerService
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC → PostgreSQL (append-only spend ledger table)
      PURPOSE: Authoritative append-only ledger of all advertising spend events.
               Each entry: eventId, adId, accountId, eventType (impression/click/
               conversion), billableAmount, currency, timestamp, pricingModel.
               Financial corrections use offset entries, never modification. (DD-169, DR-131)
      METHODS: AppendLedgerEntryAsync(entry, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[str] (entryId)
               GetLedgerAsync(accountId, dateRange, tenantId) → DataProcessResult<list[object]>
               AppendOffsetEntryAsync(originalEntryId, reason, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[str]

F831  IFraudReversalService
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC → PostgreSQL (reversal records, append-only offset entries)
      PURPOSE: Processes fraud-detected post-billing reversals (rare path — DD-177 prevents
               most fraud at gate). Creates offset entries in F830 spend ledger.
               Reversal triggers credit to advertiser account. Reversals are append-only
               records — they never delete the original billing entry.
      METHODS: ProcessReversalAsync(originalEntryId, fraudEvidence, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[str]
               GetReversalHistoryAsync(accountId, tenantId) → DataProcessResult<list[object]>

F832  IBillingEventService
      FABRIC:  QUEUE FABRIC (billing event consumer) + DATABASE FABRIC → PG
      PURPOSE: Consumes billing-eligible events from QUEUE FABRIC (fraud-screened
               impressions/clicks from F812/F813), evaluates billability (viewability
               threshold, engagement minimum), writes billable entries to F830 ledger,
               and emits budget-decrement event to F811. Central billing choke point.
      METHODS: ProcessBillingEventAsync(event, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]
               IsBillableAsync(event, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[bool]

F833  IInvoiceGeneratorService
      FABRIC:  AI ENGINE FABRIC (invoice narrative generation) + DATABASE FABRIC → PG
      PURPOSE: Generates monthly/threshold invoices from F830 spend ledger. AI ENGINE
               FABRIC produces human-readable invoice narrative and line-item descriptions.
               Invoice stored in PG; PDF generated on demand. Supports multiple currencies
               and tax jurisdictions (tax rate from FREEDOM config).
      METHODS: GenerateInvoiceAsync(accountId, billingPeriod, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[str]
               GetInvoiceAsync(invoiceId, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]

F834  IReportingService
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC → Elasticsearch (reporting aggregate index) + DW (int retention)
      PURPOSE: Aggregated campaign performance reporting: impressions, clicks, spend,
               conversions, CPM/CPC/CPA, ROAS, reach, frequency. Aggregations computed
               by background jobs and stored in ES reporting index. Near-real-time
               (10-minute lag for dashboard); daily aggregates in DW for 24+ month retention.
      METHODS: GetPerformanceReportAsync(filters, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]
               GetReachFrequencyAsync(campaignId, dateRange, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]

F835  IDeveloperAnalyticsService
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC → Elasticsearch (developer analytics index)
      PURPOSE: Provides developer-facing API usage analytics: requests per endpoint,
               error rates by code, latency percentiles, quota consumption vs limit.
               Data aggregated from F739 abuse prevention and gateway logs. Available
               in developer dashboard (F844). Near-real-time with 5-minute aggregation lag.
      METHODS: GetApiUsageAsync(appId, dateRange, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]
               GetErrorBreakdownAsync(appId, dateRange, tenantId) → DataProcessResult<list[object]>

F836  IInsightService
      FABRIC:  AI ENGINE FABRIC (insight generation) + DATABASE FABRIC → ES
      PURPOSE: AI-generated actionable insights for advertisers: "Your CPM is 40% above
               benchmark — consider adjusting targeting"; "Click-through rate dropped 12%
               — creative fatigue detected." AI ENGINE FABRIC analyses performance trends
               insights stored in ES with expiry. Insight generation is async background job.
      METHODS: GenerateInsightsAsync(accountId, tenantId) → DataProcessResult<list[object]>
               GetInsightsAsync(accountId, tenantId) → DataProcessResult<list[object]>

F837  ISpendForecastService
      FABRIC:  AI ENGINE FABRIC (time-series forecasting model) + DATABASE FABRIC → ES
      PURPOSE: Projects spend trajectory for the billing period based on: current pacing
               (F799), historical spend patterns, campaign objective, and remaining budget.
               Forecast alerts triggered when on-track to overspend or underspend by > 20%.
      METHODS: ForecastSpendAsync(accountId, billingPeriod, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]
               GetSpendAlertAsync(accountId, tenantId) → DataProcessResult<dict[str, Any]?>
```

---

# FAMILY 118 — Multi-Tenant Isolation & Governance
## F838–F851 | Scope: Audit logs, abuse detection, tenant edge resolution, quota isolation

Tenant identity resolved ONCE at edge (F846). No internal service re-resolves. (DD-172)
Per-tenant quota isolation via F849/F851. No shared quota pool. (DD-176)

```
F838  IAuditLogService
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC → Elasticsearch (audit log index, append-only)
      PURPOSE: Platform-wide audit log: "who accessed what, when, from which app."
               Every API read/write on sensitive nodes (user profile, ad accounts,
               payment methods) generates an audit entry. Audit log is append-only,
               immutable, tenant-scoped. Retention: 7 years (compliance requirement).
      METHODS: LogAccessAsync(auditEvent, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[str]
               QueryAuditAsync(filters, tenantId) → DataProcessResult<list[object]>

F839  IFlowVersionService
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC → Elasticsearch (flow version index, immutable snapshots)
      PURPOSE: Stores immutable flow version snapshots. Published FlowVersion can never
               be modified — any change creates a new version. Supports: publish, list,
               compare, rollback (rollback = publish previous version as new current).
               (DD-173, DR-132)
      METHODS: PublishVersionAsync(flowId, specSnapshot, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[str]
               GetVersionAsync(flowId, version, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]
               ListVersionsAsync(flowId, tenantId) → DataProcessResult<list[object]>
               RollbackAsync(flowId, targetVersion, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[str]

F840  IGovernancePolicyService
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC → Elasticsearch (governance policy index, FREEDOM config)
      PURPOSE: Stores and enforces platform governance policies: data retention rules,
               GDPR deletion propagation requirements, privacy policy versions accepted,
               and audit log retention thresholds. FREEDOM config — compliance team
               updates policies without code deployment.
      METHODS: GetPolicyAsync(policyType, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]
               EvaluateComplianceAsync(context, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]

F841  IAbuseDetectionService
      FABRIC:  AI ENGINE FABRIC (pattern analysis) + DATABASE FABRIC → ES (abuse signals)
      PURPOSE: Deeper abuse analysis downstream of F739 (fast-path signal detection).
               Analyses: token sharing (same token used from many IPs), scraping
               patterns (systematic enumeration of user IDs), coordinated inauthentic
               behaviour (bulk actions from multiple accounts with same fingerprint).
               Findings fed back to F739 for future fast-path blocking.
      METHODS: AnalyzeAbusePatternAsync(signals, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]
               FlagAccountAsync(accountId, abuseType, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[bool]

F842  ITokenSharingDetectorService
      FABRIC:  AI ENGINE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC → ES (token-IP correlation log)
      PURPOSE: Detects access tokens being shared across multiple IP ranges or geolocations
               inconsistent with legitimate use. Flags tokens used from >N distinct
               ASNs within a sliding time window. Flagged tokens queued for F745
               revocation review.
      METHODS: AnalyzeTokenUsageAsync(tokenId, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]
               IsTokenSharedAsync(tokenId, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[bool]

F843  IScrapingDetectorService
      FABRIC:  AI ENGINE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC → ES (request pattern log)
      PURPOSE: Detects systematic scraping: sequential node ID enumeration, high-volume
               field extraction on many distinct nodes within a short window, absence
               of human browsing patterns (no re-fetches, no variation in field
               selection). Findings reported to F739 (fast-path block) and F838 (audit log).
      METHODS: AnalyzeRequestPatternAsync(appId, window, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]
               IsScraping Async(appId, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[bool]

F844  IDeveloperDashboardService
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC → Elasticsearch (dashboard aggregation index)
      PURPOSE: Aggregates and serves developer-facing operational data: API usage metrics
               (F835), webhook delivery rates (F764), error breakdown by endpoint,
               quota utilisation vs limit, recent audit entries for their app.
               Read-only aggregation layer; no writes through this service.
      METHODS: GetDashboardAsync(appId, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]
               GetWebhookHealthAsync(appId, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]

F845  IErrorBudgetService
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC → Elasticsearch (SLO / error budget index)
      PURPOSE: Tracks SLO error budget consumption per API endpoint per tenant.
               Budget consumption = (1 - success_rate) × observation_window.
               Alerts when budget burn rate exceeds 2× sustainable rate (fast burn).
               Feeds developer dashboard (F844) and on-call alerting pipeline.
      METHODS: RecordOutcomeAsync(endpoint, success, latencyMs, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[bool]
               GetErrorBudgetAsync(endpoint, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]

F846  ITenantEdgeResolverService
      FABRIC:  CORE FABRIC (token claim extraction via MicroserviceBase auth context)
      PURPOSE: Resolves tenant identity ONCE at API edge from validated JWT claims or
               subdomain. Result placed in trusted scope context propagated to all
               downstream services. Internal services read tenantId from scope context
               ONLY — never from user-supplied headers or request body. (DD-172, DR-133)
      METHODS: ResolveTenantAsync(requestContext) → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]
               ValidateTenantContextAsync(scopeContext) → DataProcessResult[bool]
      DNA:     MicroserviceBase — scope context propagation standard component
               DataProcessResult[T] — unresolvable tenant = deny result, not exception

F847  ICrossFlowConflictService
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC → Elasticsearch (BFA index)
      PURPOSE: Registers FLOW-20 entities, events, and APIs in BFA indices for conflict
               detection against FLOW-01 through FLOW-19. Checks new ad entities against
               existing social graph entities (FLOW-01/02), content pipeline (FLOW-03/04),
               and finance engine (FLOW-13). Flags conflicts before code ships.
      METHODS: RegisterEntitiesAsync(entities, flowId, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[bool]
               CheckConflictsAsync(flowId, tenantId) → DataProcessResult<list[object]>

F848  IVersionCompatService
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC → Elasticsearch (compatibility matrix index)
      PURPOSE: Validates that new factory interfaces (F734–F851) are backward-compatible
               with existing flows (FLOW-01 through FLOW-19). Checks: no existing
               interface method signatures changed, no shared ES index schemas modified
               without migration, no QUEUE FABRIC event schema breaks.
      METHODS: ValidateCompatibilityAsync(flowId, factories, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]
               GetCompatibilityMatrixAsync(tenantId) → DataProcessResult<list[object]>

F849  IQuotaEnforcementService
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC → Redis (per-tenant quota counters)
      PURPOSE: Enforces per-tenant API quotas: requests per minute, requests per day,
               concurrent connections. Quota counters are per-tenant in Redis (not shared
               pool — DD-176). Quota limits are FREEDOM config per tenant tier.
               Quota exhaustion returns 429 result (not exception), with Retry-After header.
      METHODS: CheckQuotaAsync(tenantId, quotaType) → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]
               IncrementQuotaAsync(tenantId, quotaType) → DataProcessResult[bool]
               GetQuotaStatusAsync(tenantId) → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]

F850  IRateLimitRouterService
      FABRIC:  CORE FABRIC (MicroserviceBase cache component + Redis)
      PURPOSE: Routes rate limit enforcement decisions to the correct per-tenant quota
               enforcer (F849). Sits between F739 (fast-path abuse) and F849 (per-tenant
               quota). Provides: token bucket per endpoint, sliding window per app,
               global platform rate limit as backstop. All limits FREEDOM config.
      METHODS: CheckRateLimitAsync(requestContext, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]
               RecordRequestAsync(requestContext, tenantId) → DataProcessResult[bool]

F851  INoisyNeighborGuardService
      FABRIC:  DATABASE FABRIC → Redis (burst detection) + ES (metrics)
      PURPOSE: Detects and throttles noisy-neighbor tenants whose burst traffic is
               degrading queue/database shared-infrastructure performance for others.
               Detection: per-tenant throughput vs fair-share baseline over 60s window.
               Mitigation: soft throttle (inject backpressure token), hard throttle
               (queue events to overflow), alert on-call. (DD-176)
      METHODS: DetectBurstAsync(tenantId) → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]
               ApplyThrottleAsync(tenantId, level) → DataProcessResult[bool]
               GetNeighborMetricsAsync() → DataProcessResult<list[object]>
```

---

## DESIGN RECORDS DR-122–DR-133

### DR-122: REST Graph Paths — API Contract and Versioning Standard
```
DECISION: All public Graph API endpoints follow the pattern /{version}/{nodeId}/{edge}?fields=...
          Version prefix (v1, v2...) is mandatory. No unversioned endpoints.
          Internal query planning uses GraphQL-style federation (F742) but is NEVER
          exposed as the public API surface.
RATIONALE: Versioned graph paths enable per-endpoint rate limiting, quota tracking, and
           deprecation with sunset headers (DD-164). GraphQL introspection leaks schema.
ENFORCEMENT: F735 (version resolution), F736 (normalisation), AF-7 compliance check
             rejects any generated controller that exposes GraphQL endpoint publicly.
SCOPE:     Family 103 — all gateway services (F734–F739)
AFFECTS:   T287, T288, T299
```

### DR-123: OAuth Scope Catalog and App Review Gates
```
DECISION: Platform OAuth scopes are categorised as: basic (no review required),
          sensitive (automated review via F747), and restricted (human review mandatory,
          e.g., manage_ads, pages_manage_posts). Sensitive scope applications must pass
          AI ENGINE FABRIC review with confidence > 0.85; restricted scopes require
          human approval regardless.
RATIONALE: Sensitive scopes grant access to user data and publishing capabilities.
           Without review gates, fraudulent apps can harvest data or abuse publishing.
ENFORCEMENT: F747 (review service), F748 (scope enforcement), iron rule IR-302-1.
SCOPE:     Family 105 — Identity & OAuth (F743–F748)
AFFECTS:   T302
```

### DR-124: Partial Authorization Response Format
```
DECISION: Graph API responses for partially-authorized requests return:
          { data: { ...authorized fields }, errors: [ { path, code, message } ] }
          "errors" array contains only PERMISSION_DENIED / SCOPE_INSUFFICIENT /
          RESTRICTED / AGE_GATED codes. HTTP status: 200 (not 403) when any data returned.
RATIONALE: 403 on partial auth forces callers to implement retry with reduced field sets.
           200 with partial errors follows Graph API industry convention (DD-165, F755).
ENFORCEMENT: F755 (partial error builder), AF-9 validates response format in generated code.
SCOPE:     Family 106 — Permission Engine (F749–F756)
AFFECTS:   T287, T288, T299
```

### DR-125: HMAC-SHA256 Webhook Signature Specification
```
DECISION: All webhook deliveries include header X-Platform-Signature: sha256={hex}.
          Signature computed as HMAC-SHA256(secret, payload_bytes) where secret is
          per-subscription, stored hashed in F761, and never returned in API responses.
          Signature computed by F764 BEFORE dispatch. No unsigned delivery path exists.
RATIONALE: DD-170. HMAC allows app to verify payload authenticity without a separate
           verification API call. Industry standard across major webhook providers.
ENFORCEMENT: F764 (delivery service IRON RULE IR-289-1: DeliverAsync MUST call
             ComputeHmacAsync before any HTTP dispatch).
SCOPE:     Family 108 — Webhooks (F761–F765)
AFFECTS:   T289
```

### DR-126: PCI Tokenization Boundary
```
DECISION: IPaymentMethodService (F773) is the ONLY service that accepts raw payment
          instrument data. F773 immediately forwards to external payment vault and
          stores only the vault-issued token. No other service EVER receives or stores
          raw PAN, CVV, or full card data. All internal references use the token.
RATIONALE: DD-171. Tokenization removes the platform from PCI-DSS Level 1 scope.
           Encrypted PAN storage still requires PCI audit scope.
ENFORCEMENT: AF-8 security scan rejects any generated service that stores fields named
             "card_number", "pan", "cvv", or any pattern matching card data.
SCOPE:     Family 110 — F773 only
AFFECTS:   T290
```

### DR-127: Auction p99 Latency SLO — 50ms Budget
```
DECISION: IAuctionEngineService (F810) has a p99 latency SLO of 50ms for the
          critical-path auction execution (from EligibleAdsLoader result to winner
          determination). This SLO is enforced by architectural constraint: F810
          may ONLY read from Redis (no PG, no ES) in the auction path.
RATIONALE: DD-166. Feed render latency budget is 200ms total; ad auction cannot consume
           more than 50ms. Redis reads are sub-millisecond; PG reads would violate budget.
ENFORCEMENT: AF-7 compliance check rejects any F810 implementation that calls
             DATABASE FABRIC with provider != Redis in the RunAuctionAsync method.
SCOPE:     Family 114 — F810
AFFECTS:   T292
```

### DR-128: Consent-Before-Evaluation Protocol
```
DECISION: The targeting evaluation pipeline MUST execute in this exact order:
          1. F793 ConsentLookup → if no consent: SKIP all targeting evaluation
          2. F787 TargetingEvaluator → only runs if consent confirmed
          3. F794 ExclusionList → post-evaluation exclusion filter
          Swapping steps 1 and 2 is a BUILD FAILURE (iron rule IR-292-3).
RATIONALE: DD-167. GDPR/CCPA require that no targeting evaluation processes personal
           data without consent. Post-filter approach still processes non-consented data.
ENFORCEMENT: IR-292-3 iron rule; AF-7 checks targeting pipeline ordering in generated code.
SCOPE:     Family 113 — F787, F793, F794
AFFECTS:   T292, T301
```

### DR-129: Political Ad Dual-Gate Protocol
```
DECISION: An ad is treated as "political" if EITHER: (a) F803 classifier returns
          political confidence ≥ 0.3 OR (b) ad category explicitly set to political
          by advertiser. For political ads: BOTH F803 classifier AND F806 political
          verification MUST pass before creative is eligible for auction.
          F806 checks: advertiser has political verification for the target jurisdiction.
          Missing verification = BUILD FAILURE regardless of campaign budget or reach.
RATIONALE: DD-168. Regulatory requirements (US FICA, EU DSA) mandate explicit verification.
           Classifier confidence is probabilistic; regulatory exposure is binary.
ENFORCEMENT: IR-295-2 iron rule; AF-9 validates dual-gate in generated review pipeline.
SCOPE:     Families 69–71 — F802, F803, F806, F809
AFFECTS:   T295, T292
```

### DR-130: Redis-Only Mutable Auction State Contract
```
DECISION: The following state types MUST be sourced from Redis (not PG, not ES)
          within the IAuctionEngineService (F810) RunAuctionAsync method:
          - Frequency cap counters (F798 → Redis INCR)
          - Pacing signals (F799 → Redis hash)
          - Budget remaining (F811 counter → Redis INCR)
          - Eligibility cache (F809 → Redis TTL 5min)
          Any other state source in the auction critical path = BUILD FAILURE.
RATIONALE: DD-166. 50ms p99 SLO (DR-127) requires all hot-path reads to be Redis.
           PG/ES reads in auction path would add 5–50ms per read.
ENFORCEMENT: AF-7 compliance rejects generated code with non-Redis fabric calls in
             RunAuctionAsync. IR-292-1 iron rule.
SCOPE:     Family 114 — F795–F811
AFFECTS:   T292, T293
```

### DR-131: Append-Only Spend Ledger Schema Contract
```
DECISION: The spend ledger table (F830) schema enforces append-only at the database level:
          - PG table has no UPDATE or DELETE grants for application users
          - Application-level: AppendLedgerEntryAsync and AppendOffsetEntryAsync only
          - Schema includes: entryId (UUID), originalEntryId (FK, for offsets), entryType
            (ORIGINAL/OFFSET), billableAmount, correctionAmount (for offsets), auditReason
          - entryId is immutable (PG primary key with no update trigger)
RATIONALE: DD-169. SOC2 Type II and ISO 27001 require immutable financial records.
           Billing dispute resolution requires the original event chain plus corrections.
ENFORCEMENT: AF-8 security scan rejects any generated code calling UPDATE/DELETE on
             ledger indices. IR-294-4 iron rule.
SCOPE:     Family 117 — F830, F831
AFFECTS:   T293, T294, T296, T303
```

### DR-132: Immutable Flow Version Snapshot Contract
```
DECISION: FlowVersion documents in ES have the following immutability contract:
          - Document ID = {flowId}:{semver} (version embedded in ID — prevents overwrite)
          - ES index mapping has dynamic: false (no new fields added to existing versions)
          - IFlowVersionService (F839): no UpdateVersionAsync method exists
          - "Rollback" = PublishVersionAsync with previous spec as new current version
          - Status transitions: DRAFT → REVIEW → PUBLISHED → DEPRECATED (unidirectional)
RATIONALE: DD-173. Incident root cause analysis requires stable version history.
           Mutable specs make before/after comparison impossible.
ENFORCEMENT: AF-7 rejects generated code that calls any update/delete on flow version index.
             IR-297-2 iron rule.
SCOPE:     Family 118 — F839
AFFECTS:   T297
```

### DR-133: Attribution Window and Tenant Resolution FREEDOM Config Schemas
```
DECISION: Two canonical FREEDOM config schemas defined for FLOW-20:

          Attribution Window Config (F829 / F821):
          {
            "tenantId": "...",
            "accountId": "...",
            "clickWindowDays": 7,          # 1–90
            "viewWindowDays": 1,           # 0–7 (0 = view attribution disabled)
            "attributionModel": "last_click", # last_click|first_click|linear|data_driven
            "crossDeviceEnabled": true
          }

          Tenant Edge Resolution Context (F846):
          {
            "tenantId": "...",              # resolved from JWT claim "tid" or subdomain
            "resolvedAt": "ISO8601",
            "source": "jwt_claim|subdomain",
            "propagatedTo": ["downstream services via scope context only"]
          }

          Both schemas stored in ES FREEDOM config index; admin-editable via dashboard.
RATIONALE: DD-179 (attribution windows per advertiser), DD-172 (tenant edge-only resolution).
ENFORCEMENT: AF-7 validates FREEDOM config doc structure in generated admin services.
SCOPE:     Families 70–75 — F821, F829, F846
AFFECTS:   T298, T304, all FLOW-20 task types
```

# ENGINE ARCHITECTURE — FLOW-21: Forms & Flow Automation Builder

## OVERVIEW

```
NEW FACTORIES:  F852–F900  (49 total)
NEW FAMILIES:   119–127      (9 new families)
NEW DR:         DR-134–DR-143 (10 new design records)
DOMAIN:         Forms authoring + submission pipeline +
                automation recipes + connector adapters + MT isolation
```

All services generated by the engine MUST:
- Extend MicroserviceBase (DNA-4)
- Use dict[str, Any] via parse_document — no typed models (DNA-1)
- Return DataProcessResult[T] — never throw for business logic (DNA-3)
- Include tenantId on every DB query (DNA-5)
- Use build_search_filter — skip empty fields (DNA-2)
- Route through DynamicController — no entity-specific controllers (DNA-6)
- Resolve ALL external dependencies via CreateAsync() factory pattern

## FAMILY 119 — FORM SCHEMA & AUTHORING (F852–F859)

### F852 — IFormSchemaService
```
INTERFACE:      IFormSchemaService
FACTORY ID:     F852
FAMILY:         60 — Form Schema & Authoring
FABRIC:         DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch — schema store)
RESOLUTION:     CreateAsync(FactoryResolutionContext{provider:"elasticsearch"})
METHODS:
  StoreSchema(dict[str, Any] schema, str tenantId) → DataProcessResult[str]
  GetSchema(str formId, str tenantId) → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]
  ListSchemas(dict[str, Any] filter, str tenantId) → DataProcessResult<List<dict[str, Any]>>
  PublishSchema(str formId, str tenantId) → DataProcessResult[bool]
DNA COMPLIANCE:
  DNA-1: Schema stored as Dictionary, no typed FormSchema class
  DNA-2: build_search_filter skips empty formId/tenantId on list queries
  DNA-5: tenantId on every ES index write and search
PURPOSE:        Stores and retrieves form schema definitions (fields, pages, settings)
                as dynamic ES documents. Admin-configurable via FREEDOM layer.
```

### F853 — IFieldDefinitionService
```
INTERFACE:      IFieldDefinitionService
FACTORY ID:     F853
FAMILY:         60 — Form Schema & Authoring
FABRIC:         DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch — field catalog)
RESOLUTION:     CreateAsync(FactoryResolutionContext{provider:"elasticsearch"})
METHODS:
  RegisterFieldType(dict[str, Any] fieldDef, str tenantId) → DataProcessResult[str]
  GetFieldCatalog(str tenantId) → DataProcessResult<List<dict[str, Any]>>
  ValidateFieldConfig(dict[str, Any] config) → DataProcessResult[bool]
DNA COMPLIANCE:
  DNA-1: Field definitions as Dictionary, not typed FieldDefinition class
  DNA-5: tenantId scopes field catalog per tenant
PURPOSE:        Maintains the catalog of available field types (text, select, file,
                date, payment, repeater). Extensible at runtime via ES document.
```

### F854 — IConditionalLogicService
```
INTERFACE:      IConditionalLogicService
FACTORY ID:     F854
FAMILY:         60 — Form Schema & Authoring
FABRIC:         DATABASE FABRIC (ES rule store) + AI ENGINE FABRIC (rule suggestion)
RESOLUTION:     CreateAsync(FactoryResolutionContext{primary:"elasticsearch", ai:"dispatcher"})
METHODS:
  StoreRules(str formId, dict[str, Any] rules, str tenantId) → DataProcessResult[str]
  EvaluateRules(str formId, dict[str, Any] fieldValues, str tenantId) → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]
  SuggestRules(dict[str, Any] formSchema, str tenantId) → DataProcessResult<List<dict[str, Any]>>
DNA COMPLIANCE:
  DNA-1: Rules stored as Dictionary — no compiled code/typed models
  DNA-5: tenantId isolates rule sets
NOTE:           SuggestRules calls AI ENGINE FABRIC via IAiProvider.GenerateAsync()
                Never calls openai.chat() or anthropic.messages() directly (DR-136)
PURPOSE:        Evaluates show/hide/require field conditions at runtime from
                ES rule documents. AI suggests rules from existing schema.
```

### F855 — IFormVersioningService
```
INTERFACE:      IFormVersioningService
FACTORY ID:     F855
FAMILY:         60 — Form Schema & Authoring
FABRIC:         DATABASE FABRIC (PostgreSQL — immutable version history)
RESOLUTION:     CreateAsync(FactoryResolutionContext{provider:"postgresql"})
METHODS:
  CreateVersion(str formId, dict[str, Any] snapshot, str tenantId) → DataProcessResult[int]
  GetVersion(str formId, int version, str tenantId) → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]
  ListVersions(str formId, str tenantId) → DataProcessResult<List<dict[str, Any]>>
  RollbackVersion(str formId, int version, str tenantId) → DataProcessResult[bool]
DNA COMPLIANCE:
  DNA-3: Version conflict returns DataProcessResult.Failure, never throws
  DNA-5: tenantId on all PG queries
PURPOSE:        Maintains immutable version history for schema changes.
                Each publish creates a new version snapshot.
```

### F856 — IFormPublishService
```
INTERFACE:      IFormPublishService
FACTORY ID:     F856
FAMILY:         60 — Form Schema & Authoring
FABRIC:         QUEUE FABRIC (publish event) + DATABASE FABRIC (ES — publish state)
RESOLUTION:     CreateAsync(FactoryResolutionContext{queue:"redis-streams", db:"elasticsearch"})
METHODS:
  PublishForm(str formId, str tenantId) → DataProcessResult[str]
  UnpublishForm(str formId, str tenantId) → DataProcessResult[bool]
  GetPublishState(str formId, str tenantId) → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]
DNA COMPLIANCE:
  DNA-3: Publish failures return DataProcessResult.Failure (no exceptions)
  DNA-5: tenantId on all state reads/writes
QUEUE EVENT:    FORM_PUBLISHED emitted after ES state update (persist-before-emit, DR-134)
PURPOSE:        Manages form lifecycle: draft → published → archived.
                Triggers downstream render spec invalidation via queue.
```

```
INTERFACE:      IFormRenderSpecService

```

```
                Dedup check via Redis key (idempotencyKey = hash(formId+visitorId+payload))
```

### F867 — IAntiSpamService

```
INTERFACE:      IAntiSpamService

  CheckSpam(dict[str, Any] submission, str tenantId) → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]

  IsRateLimited(str visitorId, str formId, str tenantId) → DataProcessResult[bool]
IRON RULE:      Anti-spam runs BEFORE entry persistence. Never inline. (DR-135, CF-382)
DNA COMPLIANCE:
  DNA-1: Spam result as Dictionary {score, reason, blocked}
  DNA-3: Spam block returns DataProcessResult.Failure with spam metadata
NOTE:           Composite gate: honeypot check + reCAPTCHA verify + AI scoring (SK-195)
```

### F868 — IEntryPersistenceService
```
INTERFACE:      IEntryPersistenceService
FACTORY ID:     F868
FAMILY:         62 — Submission Pipeline
FABRIC:         DATABASE FABRIC (ES primary) + DATABASE FABRIC (PG — audit log)
RESOLUTION:     CreateAsync(FactoryResolutionContext{primary:"elasticsearch", audit:"postgresql"})
METHODS:
  PersistEntry(dict[str, Any] normalized, str formId, str tenantId) → DataProcessResult[str]
  GetEntry(str entryId, str tenantId) → DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]
  UpdateEntry(str entryId, dict[str, Any] delta, str tenantId) → DataProcessResult[bool]
IRON RULE:      Entry persisted BEFORE any event emitted. (DR-134, CF-386, INV-15-1)
DNA COMPLIANCE:
  DNA-1: Entry as Dictionary — entryId = Ulid.NewUlid()
  DNA-5: tenantId on every ES write and PG audit record
CORRECT USAGE:
  # DNA-1: Dictionary, DNA-5: tenantId, DNA-3: DataProcessResult
   document = _objectProcessor.ProcessDocument(payload)
  document["tenantId"] = tenantId
  document["entryId"] = Ulid.NewUlid().ToString()
   result = await _database.StoreDocument("entries", document)
  return result.IsSuccess
    ? DataProcessResult[str].Success(document["entryId"].ToString())
    : DataProcessResult[str].Failure(result.Error)
```

## DESIGN RECORDS (DR-134–DR-143)

### DR-134 — Persist-Before-Emit
```
DECISION:   Entry must be saved to DATABASE FABRIC before QUEUE FABRIC event fires.
RATIONALE:  If event fires before persist, downstream consumers may read non-existent
            entries. Entry is source of truth; events are notifications of state.
ENFORCEMENT: CF-386, IR-312-1, INV-15-1
VIOLATED BY: Calling IQueueService.EnqueueAsync() before IEntryPersistenceService.PersistEntry()
```

### DR-135 — Anti-Spam as Fabric Gate
```
DECISION:   IAntiSpamService is a discrete QUEUE FABRIC stage — never inline middleware.
RATIONALE:  Inline spam checks block the submission thread. Fabric gate allows
            async composite scoring (honeypot + reCAPTCHA + AI) with retry.
ENFORCEMENT: CF-382, IR-311-1, INV-15-2
VIOLATED BY: Calling spam check logic inside SubmissionIntakeService handler
```

### DR-136 — Conditional Logic as ES Document
```
DECISION:   Form conditional logic (show/hide/require) stored as ES rule documents.
            Never compiled into service code or hardcoded into generated classes.
RATIONALE:  Admin must be able to change conditions without a deployment.
            FREEDOM layer config. AI can suggest rules via IAiProvider.
ENFORCEMENT: DNA-1, AF-7, SK-199
VIOLATED BY: if (fieldValue == "yes") { showField = true; } in generated service
```

### DR-137 — Merge Tags as Runtime Resolver
```
DECISION:   Notification merge tags resolved at runtime via IMergeTagResolverService,
            with AI fallback for dynamic content. Not compiled Handlebars/Mustache.
RATIONALE:  Tag sources change (new form fields, user profile data, payment data).
            Runtime resolution + AI extension handles unknown tag types gracefully.
ENFORCEMENT: SK-191, DD-184
VIOLATED BY: Template.Replace("{submitterEmail}", entry.Email) — typed model access
```

### DR-138 — Feed Execution Idempotent via Redis Key
```
DECISION:   Every feed execution registers a Redis idempotency key before running.
            Key = hash(feedId + entryId + timestamp-window).
RATIONALE:  QUEUE FABRIC at-least-once delivery can replay events. Duplicate
            CRM contact creation or duplicate webhook sends are business failures.
ENFORCEMENT: CF-388, IR-315-2, INV-15-4, SK-192
VIOLATED BY: Feed executed without checking/registering Redis idempotency key
```

### DR-139 — Recipe DAG Stored in ES
```
DECISION:   Automation recipe definitions (trigger + steps + conditions) stored as
            ES documents. Execution engine reads DAG from ES — same pattern as
            FLOW ENGINE FABRIC (SK-392 (RagStrategyRegistry) IFlowOrchestrator).
RATIONALE:  Recipes are admin-configurable FREEDOM layer constructs.
            No code deployment required to add/modify automation recipes.
ENFORCEMENT: SK-193, DD-186
VIOLATED BY: Hardcoding recipe steps in RecipeExecutionService class body
```

### DR-140 — Connector OAuth Tokens in PG Encrypted Only
```
DECISION:   OAuth access tokens, refresh tokens, and API keys stored ONLY in
            PostgreSQL with column-level encryption. Never in ES, never in Redis.
RATIONALE:  ES is not appropriate for secrets (no column encryption).
            Redis is ephemeral — token loss = broken integration.
            PG encrypted + RLS provides strongest isolation + durability.
ENFORCEMENT: CF-395, IR-322-1, INV-15-5
VIOLATED BY: Storing token in ES document or Redis key
```

### DR-141 — File Virus Scan Before Signed URL Generation
```
DECISION:   ISignedUrlService.GenerateSignedUrl() may only be called AFTER
            IVirusScanService.MarkClean() has completed for the file.
RATIONALE:  Distributing signed URLs for unscanned files creates malware
            distribution vectors. Scan gate is enforced at the service boundary.
ENFORCEMENT: CF-401, IR-324-1, INV-15-6
VIOLATED BY: Generating signed URL immediately after upload without scan gate
```

### DR-142 — Partial Entry Redis TTL → PG Promotion
```
DECISION:   Save-and-continue partial entries stored in Redis with TTL ≤ 48 hours.
            On form completion, partial promoted to full PG entry.
            On TTL expiry, partial deleted without promotion.
RATIONALE:  Redis provides low-latency read/write for active sessions.
            PG provides durable storage for completed entries only.
            48-hour TTL prevents Redis memory bloat from abandoned sessions.
ENFORCEMENT: IR-313-3, INV-15-7, SK-198
VIOLATED BY: Storing partial entries directly in PG without Redis interim stage
```

### DR-143 — Forms Tenant Isolation Inherits FLOW-08 + FLOW-14 MT Models
```
DECISION:   IFormTenantIsolationService (F900) inherits isolation patterns from
            FLOW-08 (core MT model) and FLOW-14 (warehouse MT model, F461).
            Does not reinvent MT isolation — extends existing proven model.
RATIONALE:  Consistent MT isolation across all flows prevents isolation bypasses.
            New flows that reinvent isolation introduce inconsistent boundaries.
ENFORCEMENT: CF-398-234, SK-200, DD-190
VIOLATED BY: Writing new tenant isolation logic not based on FLOW-08/FLOW-14 patterns
```

| QUEUE FABRIC | SK-383 (RedisStreamQueue) — IQueueService | EnqueueAsync/DequeueAsync/AcknowledgeAsync |

| FLOW ENGINE FABRIC | SK-391/SK-392 (FlowOrchestrator/RagStrategyRegistry) — IFlowDefinition + IFlowOrchestrator | DAG in ES |

```
DNA-1  parse_document        dict[str, Any] — NEVER typed models
DNA-2  build_search_filter    empty fields auto-skipped
DNA-3  DataProcessResult[T] NEVER throw for business logic
DNA-4  MicroserviceBase     19 components — ALL services
DNA-5  Scope Isolation      tenantId on EVERY query/cache/index
DNA-6  DynamicController    no entity-specific controllers
```

```
  IR-908-3: publishAt uses RFC 3339 format (DD-194)
```

### F914 — IAiWritingAssistantService
```
FABRIC:     AI ENGINE FABRIC (SK-386 (AiDispatcher)) → AiDispatcher (multi-model)
RESOLUTION: CreateAsync(ctx{tenantId})  Config: content_authoring.ai_writing.ai_provider
METHODS:    SuggestContinuationAsync / GenerateSeoSummaryAsync / CheckToneAsync / EnhanceBlockAsync
IRON RULES:
  IR-914-1: AI suggestions always user-confirmable — engine never auto-commits AI output
  IR-914-2: model selection config-driven (FREEDOM: ai_writing.model_preference)
```

### F915 — ICommentThreadService
```
FABRIC:     DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → Elasticsearch
RESOLUTION: CreateAsync(ctx{tenantId})
METHODS:    AddCommentAsync / ResolveThreadAsync / GetThreadsAsync / ModerateAsync

  IR-915-1: moderation events enqueued through QUEUE FABRIC
  IR-915-2: threadId scoped by tenantId
```

### F916 — IEditorialWorkflowService
```
FABRIC:     FLOW ENGINE FABRIC (SK-391/SK-392 (FlowOrchestrator/RagStrategyRegistry)) → IFlowOrchestrator
RESOLUTION: CreateAsync(ctx{tenantId})
METHODS:    SubmitForReviewAsync / ApproveAsync / RequestRevisionAsync / GetWorkflowStateAsync

  IR-916-2: state changes generate audit events through QUEUE FABRIC
```

## FAMILY 131 — PUBLISHING PIPELINE

### F917 — IPublishJobService
```
FABRIC:     QUEUE FABRIC (SK-383 (RedisStreamQueue)) → Redis Streams
RESOLUTION: CreateAsync(ctx{tenantId, siteId})  Config: publishing.publish_job.queue_provider
METHODS:    EnqueuePublishAsync / EnqueueScheduledAsync / CancelJobAsync / GetJobStatusAsync
IRON RULES:
  IR-917-1: publish operations MUST be idempotent (idempotency-key in job payload)
  IR-917-2: scheduled publish uses RFC 3339 timestamp (DD-194)
  IR-917-3: job envelope MUST use CloudEvents format (DD-194)
```

### F918 — ICacheInvalidationService
```
FABRIC:     QUEUE FABRIC → Redis Streams + DATABASE FABRIC → Redis
RESOLUTION: CreateAsync(ctx{tenantId})
METHODS:    InvalidatePageAsync / InvalidateSiteAsync / InvalidateTokensAsync / GetInvalidationLogAsync
IRON RULES:
  IR-918-1: cache keys always include tenantId prefix
  IR-918-2: invalidation events broadcast via QUEUE FABRIC before Redis flush
  IR-918-3: design token update MUST trigger full site invalidation (CF-406)
```

### F919 — IStaticBuildService
```
FABRIC:     AI ENGINE FABRIC (SK-386 (AiDispatcher)) → AiDispatcher (build optimization)
RESOLUTION: CreateAsync(ctx{tenantId, siteId})  Config: publishing.static_build.ai_provider
METHODS:    TriggerBuildAsync / GetBuildStatusAsync / RollbackBuildAsync / GetBuildArtifactsAsync
IRON RULES:
  IR-919-1: published content = SSG path — preview = SSR path (DD-193)
  IR-919-2: build artifacts immutable — rollback = switch pointer, not delete
  IR-919-3: every build tagged with tenantId + siteId + timestamp
```

### F920 — IRenderRuntimeService
```
FABRIC:     DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → Elasticsearch
RESOLUTION: CreateAsync(ctx{tenantId, siteId})
METHODS:    RenderPageAsync / RenderTemplateAsync / GetRenderMetricsAsync
IRON RULES:
  IR-920-1: SSR render MUST NOT use cached SSG artifact (DD-193)
  IR-920-2: render errors return DataProcessResult — never unhandled 500
```

### F921 — ISitemapRssService
```
FABRIC:     DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → Elasticsearch
RESOLUTION: CreateAsync(ctx{tenantId, siteId})
METHODS:    GenerateSitemapAsync / GenerateRssAsync / InvalidateSitemapAsync
IRON RULES:
  IR-921-1: sitemap regenerated on every publish event (F917 event via QUEUE FABRIC)
  IR-921-2: sitemap scoped by tenantId + siteId
```

### F922 — IPublishRollbackService
```
FABRIC:     DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → PostgreSQL (version journal)
RESOLUTION: CreateAsync(ctx{tenantId})
METHODS:    GetPublishHistoryAsync / RollbackToVersionAsync / CreateCheckpointAsync
IRON RULES:
  IR-922-1: rollback is non-destructive — creates new "rollback publish" version
  IR-922-2: requires RBAC permission: publish.rollback (FREEDOM config)
```

## FAMILY 132 — THEME / DESIGN SYSTEM

### F923 — IDesignTokenService
```
FABRIC:     DATABASE FABRIC → Elasticsearch (store) + Redis (hot cache)
RESOLUTION: CreateAsync(ctx{tenantId, siteId})  Config: design_system.tokens.db_provider
METHODS:    GetTokensAsync / SetTokenAsync / GetTokensByThemeAsync / ExportCssVariablesAsync
IRON RULES:
  IR-923-1: token update MUST trigger cache invalidation via F918 (CF-406)
  IR-923-2: tokens stored as flat Dictionary — no nested typed ThemeConfig
  IR-923-3: token change events published to QUEUE FABRIC for downstream rebuild
```

### F924 — IGlobalStyleService

```
FABRIC:     DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → Elasticsearch
RESOLUTION: CreateAsync(ctx{tenantId})
METHODS:    GetGlobalStylesAsync / SetGlobalStyleAsync / GetTypographyScaleAsync / PropagateStyleChangeAsync

  IR-924-2: per-block override validated against global style guardrails
```

### F925 — IThemeRegistryService
```
FABRIC:     DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → Elasticsearch
RESOLUTION: CreateAsync(ctx{tenantId})
METHODS:    RegisterThemeAsync / ApplyThemeAsync / ForkThemeAsync / ListThemesAsync
IRON RULES:

  IR-925-2: theme application triggers token propagation via F923
```

### F926 — IDesignTokenRebuildService
```
FABRIC:     QUEUE FABRIC (SK-383 (RedisStreamQueue)) → Redis Streams
RESOLUTION: CreateAsync(ctx{tenantId})
METHODS:    TriggerRebuildAsync / GetRebuildStatusAsync / GetAffectedPagesAsync
IRON RULES:
  IR-926-1: rebuild scope from token dependency graph (F923 maintains)
  IR-926-2: partial rebuild supported — only affected pages
```

## FAMILY 133 — ROUTING / URLS / SEO

### F927 — ISlugService
```
FABRIC:     DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → Elasticsearch
RESOLUTION: CreateAsync(ctx{tenantId, siteId})
METHODS:    RegisterSlugAsync / ResolveSlugAsync / ChangeSlugAsync / GetSlugHistoryAsync

  IR-927-1: slug change MUST auto-create 301 redirect via F928 (CF-410)
  IR-927-2: slug uniqueness enforced per siteId + tenantId
  IR-927-3: slug format: lowercase, hyphen-only, no trailing slash
```

### F928 — IRedirectService
```
FABRIC:     DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → Redis (hot read path)
RESOLUTION: CreateAsync(ctx{tenantId, siteId})
METHODS:    CreateRedirectAsync / ResolveRedirectAsync / DeleteRedirectAsync / GetAllRedirectsAsync
IRON RULES:
  IR-928-1: redirect type (301/302) config-driven (FREEDOM)
  IR-928-2: circular redirect chains rejected (CF-411)
  IR-928-3: redirect entries keyed by tenantId + siteId
```

### F929 — ISeoMetadataService
```
FABRIC:     AI ENGINE FABRIC (SK-386 (AiDispatcher)) → AiDispatcher + DATABASE FABRIC → Elasticsearch
RESOLUTION: CreateAsync(ctx{tenantId})
METHODS:    GenerateSeoMetaAsync / GetSeoMetaAsync / SetSeoMetaAsync / ValidateSeoAsync
IRON RULES:
  IR-929-1: AI-generated SEO meta requires user confirmation
  IR-929-2: OG image stored as URL reference — never embedded base64
```

### F930 — IPermalinkRuleService
```
FABRIC:     DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → Redis
RESOLUTION: CreateAsync(ctx{tenantId, siteId})
METHODS:    GetRuleAsync / SetRuleAsync / ResolvePermalinkAsync
IRON RULES:
  IR-930-1: permalink patterns config-driven (FREEDOM: routing.permalink_patterns)
  IR-930-2: pattern change requires bulk redirect generation via F928 + F917
```

### F931 — IRobotsService
```
FABRIC:     DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → Elasticsearch
RESOLUTION: CreateAsync(ctx{tenantId})
METHODS:    GetRobotsAsync / SetRobotsRuleAsync / GenerateRobotsAsync
IRON RULES:
  IR-931-1: robots.txt scoped per siteId + tenantId
```

## CROSS-CUTTING FACTORIES (F937–F944)

| Factory | Interface | Fabric | Provider | Purpose |
|---------|-----------|--------|----------|---------|
| F937 | IWorkspaceService | DATABASE FABRIC | PostgreSQL | Multi-tenant workspace/site management |
| F938 | ISiteConfigService | DATABASE FABRIC | Elasticsearch | Site-level FREEDOM config |
| F939 | IRbacSiteService | DATABASE FABRIC | Elasticsearch | RBAC within site context |
| F940 | IAuditLogService | QUEUE + DATABASE | Redis Streams + PG | Append-only audit trail |
| F941 | ISchedulerService | QUEUE FABRIC | Redis Streams | Time-based publish trigger |
| F942 | ISearchIndexService | DATABASE + RAG | ES + Vector | Full-text + semantic search |
| F943 | IAnalyticsEventService | QUEUE FABRIC | Redis Streams | Page view/engagement events |
| F944 | ITenantProvisioningService | FLOW ENGINE FABRIC | IFlowOrchestrator | New tenant provisioning DAG |

**Iron Rules F937–F944 (shared)**:
- IR-937-1: workspace creation provisions tenantId — never shared
- IR-938-1: all FREEDOM values stored in ES — never in code
- IR-939-1: role assignments are (tenantId, siteId, userId, role) composite
- IR-940-1: audit events immutable — append-only
- IR-941-1: schedule time as RFC 3339 str; idempotency-key required
- IR-942-1: index updated via QUEUE FABRIC from publish pipeline; ALL docs include tenantId
- IR-943-1: analytics events must not include PII without consent config
- IR-944-1: provisioning idempotent; IR-944-2: DAG stored in ES (SK-391 (FlowOrchestrator))
